built with pdoc

toyopuc

Public package entry points for toyopuc.

Use:

  • ToyopucClient for low-level protocol-oriented access
  • ToyopucHighLevelClient for string-address based access
  • resolve_device() when you want address-family resolution without I/O
 1"""Public package entry points for `toyopuc`.
 2
 3Use:
 4- `ToyopucClient` for low-level protocol-oriented access
 5- `ToyopucHighLevelClient` for string-address based access
 6- `resolve_device()` when you want address-family resolution without I/O
 7"""
 8
 9from .client import ToyopucClient
10from .exceptions import ToyopucError, ToyopucProtocolError, ToyopucTimeoutError
11from .address import (
12    parse_address,
13    parse_prefixed_address,
14    encode_word_address,
15    encode_byte_address,
16    encode_bit_address,
17    encode_program_word_address,
18    encode_program_byte_address,
19    encode_program_bit_address,
20    encode_exno_bit_u32,
21    encode_exno_byte_u32,
22    split_u32_words,
23    encode_ext_no_address,
24    fr_block_ex_no,
25    encode_fr_word_addr32,
26)
27from .high_level import ResolvedDevice, ToyopucHighLevelClient, resolve_device
28from .protocol import ClockData, CpuStatusData
29from .relay import RelayLayer, format_relay_hop, normalize_relay_hops, parse_relay_hops
30

32    'ToyopucClient',
33    'ToyopucHighLevelClient',

class ToyopucClient: 
178class ToyopucClient:
179    """Low-level TOYOPUC computer-link client.
180
181    Use this class when you want explicit control over command families,
182    numeric addresses, and transport settings. For string-address driven use,
183    prefer `ToyopucHighLevelClient`.
184    """
185    def __init__(
186        self,
187        host: str,
188        port: int,
189        *,
190        local_port: int = 0,
191        protocol: str = 'tcp',
192        timeout: float = 3.0,
193        retries: int = 0,
194        retry_delay: float = 0.2,
195        recv_bufsize: int = 8192,
196    ) -> None:
197        self.host = host
198        self.port = port
199        self.local_port = int(local_port)
200        self.protocol = protocol.lower()
201        self.timeout = timeout
202        self.retries = max(0, int(retries))
203        self.retry_delay = float(retry_delay)
204        self.recv_bufsize = recv_bufsize
205        self._sock: Optional[socket.socket] = None
206        self._last_tx: Optional[bytes] = None
207        self._last_rx: Optional[bytes] = None
208        self._fr_wait_prefers_a0: Optional[bool] = None
209        self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0: Optional[bool] = None
210
211    def __enter__(self) -> Self:
212        self.connect()
213        return self
214
215    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:
216        self.close()
217
218    def connect(self) -> None:
219        if self._sock:
220            return
221        if self.protocol == 'tcp':
222            sock = socket.create_connection((self.host, self.port), self.timeout)
223        elif self.protocol == 'udp':
224            sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
225            if self.local_port:
226                sock.bind(('', self.local_port))
227            sock.settimeout(self.timeout)
228        else:
229            raise ValueError("protocol must be 'tcp' or 'udp'")
230        self._sock = sock
231
232    def close(self) -> None:
233        if self._sock:
234            try:
235                self._sock.close()
236            finally:
237                self._sock = None
238        self._last_tx = None
239        self._last_rx = None
240
241    @property
242    def last_tx(self) -> Optional[bytes]:
243        return self._last_tx
244
245    @property
246    def last_rx(self) -> Optional[bytes]:
247        return self._last_rx
248
249    def _recv_exact(self, n: int) -> bytes:
250        assert self._sock is not None
251        chunks = []
252        remaining = n
253        while remaining > 0:
254            try:
255                chunk = self._sock.recv(remaining)
256            except socket.timeout as e:
257                raise ToyopucTimeoutError('Receive timeout') from e
258            if not chunk:
259                raise ToyopucProtocolError('Connection closed while receiving')
260            chunks.append(chunk)
261            remaining -= len(chunk)
262        return b''.join(chunks)
263
264    def _send_and_recv(self, payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
265        attempt = 0
266        last_err: Optional[Exception] = None
267        while attempt <= self.retries:
268            attempt += 1
269            if not self._sock:
270                self.connect()
271            assert self._sock is not None
272            self._last_tx = payload
273            self._last_rx = None
274
275            try:
276                if self.protocol == 'tcp':
277                    self._sock.sendall(payload)
278                    header = self._recv_exact(4)
279                    ll, lh = header[2], header[3]
280                    length = ll | (lh << 8)
281                    body = self._recv_exact(length)
282                    frame = header + body
283                else:
284                    self._sock.sendto(payload, (self.host, self.port))
285                    frame, _ = self._sock.recvfrom(self.recv_bufsize)
286            except socket.timeout as e:
287                last_err = ToyopucTimeoutError('Send/receive timeout')
288                if attempt <= self.retries:
289                    try:
290                        self.close()
291                    except Exception:
292                        pass
293                    import time
294
295                    time.sleep(self.retry_delay)
296                    continue
297                raise last_err from e
298            except OSError as e:
299                last_err = ToyopucError('Socket error')
300                if attempt <= self.retries:
301                    try:
302                        self.close()
303                    except Exception:
304                        pass
305                    import time
306
307                    time.sleep(self.retry_delay)
308                    continue
309                raise last_err from e
310
311            self._last_rx = frame
312            resp = parse_response(frame)
313            if resp.ft != FT_RESPONSE:
314                raise ToyopucProtocolError(f'Unexpected frame type: 0x{resp.ft:02X}')
315            if resp.rc != 0x00:
316                raise ToyopucError(format_response_error(resp))
317            return resp
318
319        if last_err:
320            raise last_err
321        raise ToyopucError('Send/receive failed')
322
323    def send_raw(self, cmd: int, data: bytes = b'') -> ResponseFrame:
324        payload = build_command(cmd, data)
325        return self._send_and_recv(payload)
326
327    def send_payload(self, payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
328        """Send a fully-built command payload and return the parsed response."""
329        return self._send_and_recv(payload)
330
331    def read_words(self, addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
332        """Read one or more basic-area words with `CMD=1C`."""
333        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_word_read(addr, count))
334        if resp.cmd != 0x1C:
335            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
336        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)
337
338    def write_words(self, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
339        """Write one or more basic-area words with `CMD=1D`."""
340        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_word_write(addr, values))
341        if resp.cmd != 0x1D:
342            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
343
344    def read_bytes(self, addr: int, count: int) -> bytes:
345        """Read one or more basic-area bytes with `CMD=1E`."""
346        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_byte_read(addr, count))
347        if resp.cmd != 0x1E:
348            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
349        return resp.data
350
351    def write_bytes(self, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
352        """Write one or more basic-area bytes with `CMD=1F`."""
353        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_byte_write(addr, values))
354        if resp.cmd != 0x1F:
355            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
356
357    def read_bit(self, addr: int) -> bool:
358        """Read one basic-area bit with `CMD=20`."""
359        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_bit_read(addr))
360        if resp.cmd != 0x20:
361            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
362        if len(resp.data) != 1:
363            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Bit read response must be 1 byte')
364        return resp.data[0] != 0
365
366    def write_bit(self, addr: int, value: bool) -> None:
367        """Write one basic-area bit with `CMD=21`."""
368        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_bit_write(addr, 1 if value else 0))
369        if resp.cmd != 0x21:
370            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
371
372    def read_words_multi(self, addrs: Iterable[int]) -> List[int]:
373        """Read multiple non-contiguous basic-area words with `CMD=22`."""
374        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_word_read(addrs))
375        if resp.cmd != 0x22:
376            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
377        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)
378
379    def write_words_multi(self, pairs: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> None:
380        """Write multiple non-contiguous basic-area words with `CMD=23`."""
381        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_word_write(pairs))
382        if resp.cmd != 0x23:
383            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
384
385    def read_bytes_multi(self, addrs: Iterable[int]) -> bytes:
386        """Read multiple non-contiguous basic-area bytes with `CMD=24`."""
387        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_byte_read(addrs))
388        if resp.cmd != 0x24:
389            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
390        return resp.data
391
392    def write_bytes_multi(self, pairs: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> None:
393        """Write multiple non-contiguous basic-area bytes with `CMD=25`."""
394        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_byte_write(pairs))
395        if resp.cmd != 0x25:
396            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
397
398    def read_ext_words(self, no: int, addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
399        """Read extended-area words with `CMD=94` using `(No., addr)`."""
400        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_word_read(no, addr, count))
401        if resp.cmd != 0x94:
402            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
403        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)
404
405    def write_ext_words(self, no: int, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
406        """Write extended-area words with `CMD=95` using `(No., addr)`."""
407        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_word_write(no, addr, values))
408        if resp.cmd != 0x95:
409            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
410
411    def read_ext_bytes(self, no: int, addr: int, count: int) -> bytes:
412        """Read extended-area bytes with `CMD=96` using `(No., addr)`."""
413        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_byte_read(no, addr, count))
414        if resp.cmd != 0x96:
415            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
416        return resp.data
417
418    def write_ext_bytes(self, no: int, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
419        """Write extended-area bytes with `CMD=97` using `(No., addr)`."""
420        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_byte_write(no, addr, values))
421        if resp.cmd != 0x97:
422            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
423
424    def read_ext_multi(
425        self,
426        bit_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]],
427        byte_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
428        word_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
429    ) -> bytes:
430        """Read mixed extended points with `CMD=98`.
431
432        `bit_points` items are `(no, bit_no, addr)`.
433        `byte_points` items are `(no, addr)`.
434        `word_points` items are `(no, addr)`.
435        """
436        resp = self._send_and_recv(
437            build_ext_multi_read(list(bit_points), list(byte_points), list(word_points))
438        )
439        if resp.cmd != 0x98:
440            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
441        return resp.data
442
443    def write_ext_multi(
444        self,
445        bit_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int, int]],
446        byte_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]],
447        word_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]],
448    ) -> None:
449        """Write mixed extended points with `CMD=99`."""
450        resp = self._send_and_recv(
451            build_ext_multi_write(list(bit_points), list(byte_points), list(word_points))
452        )
453        if resp.cmd != 0x99:
454            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
455
456    def pc10_block_read(self, addr32: int, count: int) -> bytes:
457        """Read PC10 block data with `CMD=C2` from a 32-bit byte address."""
458        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_block_read(addr32, count))
459        if resp.cmd != 0xC2:
460            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
461        return resp.data
462
463    def pc10_block_write(self, addr32: int, data_bytes: bytes) -> None:
464        """Write PC10 block data with `CMD=C3` to a 32-bit byte address."""
465        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_block_write(addr32, data_bytes))
466        if resp.cmd != 0xC3:
467            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
468
469    def pc10_multi_read(self, payload: bytes) -> bytes:
470        """Read PC10 multi-point data with `CMD=C4` using a prebuilt payload."""
471        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_multi_read(payload))
472        if resp.cmd != 0xC4:
473            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
474        return resp.data
475
476    def pc10_multi_write(self, payload: bytes) -> None:
477        """Write PC10 multi-point data with `CMD=C5` using a prebuilt payload."""
478        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_multi_write(payload))
479        if resp.cmd != 0xC5:
480            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
481
482    def read_fr_words(self, index: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
483        """Read FR words via PC10 block read (`CMD=C2`).
484
485        `FR` real-hardware access uses 32-bit PC10 addressing with
486        `Ex No.=0x40-0x7F`, not `CMD=94`.
487        """
488        values: List[int] = []
489        for chunk_index, chunk_words in _iter_fr_io_segments(index, count):
490            data = self.pc10_block_read(encode_fr_word_addr32(chunk_index), chunk_words * 2)
491            values.extend(unpack_u16_le(data))
492        return values
493
494    def write_fr_words(self, index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False) -> None:
495        """Write FR words via PC10 block write (`CMD=C3`).
496
497        This updates the FR work area. Persisting the block to flash requires
498        `CMD=CA`. Set `commit=True` to commit every affected FR block after the
499        write completes.
500        """
501        self.write_fr_words_ex(index, values, commit=commit, wait=commit)
502
503    def write_fr_words_ex(
504        self,
505        index: int,
506        values: Iterable[int],
507        *,
508        commit: bool = False,
509        wait: bool = False,
510        timeout: float = 30.0,
511        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
512    ) -> None:
513        """Write FR words, with optional commit and completion wait.
514
515        When ``commit=True``, this follows the manual's 64-kbyte block unit:
516        each affected FR block is written with `C3`, committed with `CA`, and
517        optionally waited on before moving to the next block.
518        """
519        vals = [int(v) & 0xFFFF for v in values]
520        if not vals:
521            raise ValueError('values must contain at least one word')
522        offset = 0
523        for block_index, block_words in _iter_fr_segments(index, len(vals)):
524            block_offset = 0
525            while block_offset < block_words:
526                chunk_words = min(block_words - block_offset, _FR_IO_CHUNK_WORDS)
527                chunk_vals = vals[offset : offset + chunk_words]
528                data = b''.join(v.to_bytes(2, 'little') for v in chunk_vals)
529                self.pc10_block_write(encode_fr_word_addr32(block_index + block_offset), data)
530                offset += chunk_words
531                block_offset += chunk_words
532            if commit:
533                self.commit_fr_block(
534                    block_index,
535                    wait=wait,
536                    timeout=timeout,
537                    poll_interval=poll_interval,
538                )
539
540    def commit_fr_block(
541        self,
542        index: int,
543        *,
544        wait: bool = False,
545        timeout: float = 30.0,
546        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
547    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
548        """Commit the FR block containing `index` via `CMD=CA`.
549
550        By default this waits until `Data7.bit4` (`under_writing_flash_register`)
551        clears and raises on `Data7.bit5` (`abnormal_write_flash_register`).
552        """
553        self.fr_register(fr_block_ex_no(index))
554        if wait:
555            return self.wait_fr_write_complete(timeout=timeout, poll_interval=poll_interval)
556        return None
557
558    def commit_fr_range(
559        self,
560        index: int,
561        count: int = 1,
562        *,
563        wait: bool = False,
564        timeout: float = 30.0,
565        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
566    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
567        """Commit every FR block touched by a contiguous word range."""
568        last_status: Optional[CpuStatusData] = None
569        for block_index in _fr_commit_blocks(index, count):
570            last_status = self.commit_fr_block(
571                block_index,
572                wait=wait,
573                timeout=timeout,
574                poll_interval=poll_interval,
575            )
576        return last_status
577
578    def write_fr_words_committed(self, index: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
579        """Write FR words and commit every affected FR block."""
580        self.write_fr_words_ex(index, values, commit=True, wait=True)
581
582    def fr_register(self, ex_no: int) -> None:
583        """Issue the FR-register command `CMD=CA`."""
584        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_fr_register(ex_no))
585        if resp.cmd != 0xCA:
586            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
587
588    def relay_command(self, link_no: int, station_no: int, inner_payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
589        """Wrap a command in one relay hop using `CMD=60`."""
590        return self._send_and_recv(build_relay_command(link_no, station_no, inner_payload))
591
592    def relay_nested(self, hops: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], inner_payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
593        """Wrap a command in multiple relay hops using nested `CMD=60` frames."""
594        return self._send_and_recv(build_relay_nested(list(hops), inner_payload))
595
596    def send_via_relay(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], inner_payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
597        """Send a command through relay hops and return the final inner response."""
598        outer = self.relay_nested(normalize_relay_hops(hops), inner_payload)
599        layers, final = unwrap_relay_response_chain(outer)
600        if final is None:
601            last = layers[-1]
602            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
603                f"Relay NAK at link=0x{last.link_no:02X}, station=0x{last.station_no:04X}, ack=0x{last.ack:02X}"
604            )
605        return final
606
607    def relay_read_words(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
608        """Read one or more basic-area words through relay hops."""
609        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_word_read(addr, count))
610        if resp.cmd != 0x1C:
611            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay word-read response')
612        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)
613
614    def relay_write_words(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
615        """Write one or more basic-area words through relay hops."""
616        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_word_write(addr, values))
617        if resp.cmd != 0x1D:
618            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay word-write response')
619
620    def relay_read_clock(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> ClockData:
621        """Read the CPU clock through relay hops."""
622        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_clock_read())
623        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
624            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay clock response')
625        try:
626            return parse_clock_data(resp.data)
627        except Exception as e:
628            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
629                f'Failed to parse relay clock response data={resp.data.hex()}'
630            ) from e
631
632    def relay_write_clock(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], value: datetime) -> None:
633        """Set the CPU clock through relay hops via `CMD=32 / 71 00`."""
634        weekday = (value.weekday() + 1) % 7
635        resp = self.send_via_relay(
636            hops,
637            build_clock_write(
638                value.second,
639                value.minute,
640                value.hour,
641                value.day,
642                value.month,
643                value.year % 100,
644                weekday,
645            ),
646        )
647        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
648            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay clock-write response')
649        if resp.data != bytes([0x71, 0x00]):
650            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected relay clock-write response body')
651
652    def relay_read_cpu_status(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> CpuStatusData:
653        """Read the 8-byte CPU status block through relay hops."""
654        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_cpu_status_read())
655        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
656            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay CPU status response')
657        try:
658            return parse_cpu_status_data(resp.data)
659        except Exception as e:
660            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
661                f'Failed to parse relay CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
662            ) from e
663
664    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> bytes:
665        """Read raw 8-byte CPU status through relay hops via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`."""
666        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_cpu_status_read_a0())
667        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
668            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay A0 CPU status response')
669        try:
670            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0_raw(resp.data)
671        except Exception as e:
672            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
673                f'Failed to parse relay A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
674            ) from e
675
676    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> CpuStatusData:
677        """Read decoded CPU status through relay hops via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`."""
678        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_cpu_status_read_a0())
679        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
680            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay A0 CPU status response')
681        try:
682            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0(resp.data)
683        except Exception as e:
684            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
685                f'Failed to parse relay A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
686            ) from e
687
688    def relay_write_fr_words(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False) -> None:
689        """Write FR words through relay hops, optionally committing touched blocks."""
690        self.relay_write_fr_words_ex(hops, index, values, commit=commit, wait=commit)
691
692    def relay_write_fr_words_ex(
693        self,
694        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
695        index: int,
696        values: Iterable[int],
697        *,
698        commit: bool = False,
699        wait: bool = False,
700        timeout: float = 30.0,
701        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
702    ) -> None:
703        """Write FR words through relay hops, with optional commit and completion wait."""
704        vals = [int(v) & 0xFFFF for v in values]
705        if not vals:
706            raise ValueError('values must contain at least one word')
707        offset = 0
708        for block_index, block_words in _iter_fr_segments(index, len(vals)):
709            block_offset = 0
710            while block_offset < block_words:
711                chunk_words = min(block_words - block_offset, _FR_IO_CHUNK_WORDS)
712                chunk_vals = vals[offset : offset + chunk_words]
713                data = b''.join(v.to_bytes(2, 'little') for v in chunk_vals)
714                resp = self.send_via_relay(
715                    hops,
716                    build_pc10_block_write(encode_fr_word_addr32(block_index + block_offset), data),
717                )
718                if resp.cmd != 0xC3:
719                    raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay FR block-write response')
720                offset += chunk_words
721                block_offset += chunk_words
722            if commit:
723                self.relay_commit_fr_block(
724                    hops,
725                    block_index,
726                    wait=wait,
727                    timeout=timeout,
728                    poll_interval=poll_interval,
729                )
730
731    def relay_fr_register(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], ex_no: int) -> None:
732        """Issue the FR-register command `CMD=CA` through relay hops."""
733        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_fr_register(ex_no))
734        if resp.cmd != 0xCA:
735            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay FR-register response')
736
737    def relay_commit_fr_block(
738        self,
739        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
740        index: int,
741        *,
742        wait: bool = False,
743        timeout: float = 30.0,
744        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
745    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
746        """Commit the remote FR block containing `index` via relay `CMD=CA`."""
747        self.relay_fr_register(hops, fr_block_ex_no(index))
748        if wait:
749            return self.relay_wait_fr_write_complete(hops, timeout=timeout, poll_interval=poll_interval)
750        return None
751
752    def relay_commit_fr_range(
753        self,
754        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
755        index: int,
756        count: int = 1,
757        *,
758        wait: bool = False,
759        timeout: float = 30.0,
760        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
761    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
762        """Commit every FR block touched by a contiguous relay FR range."""
763        last_status: Optional[CpuStatusData] = None
764        for block_index in _fr_commit_blocks(index, count):
765            last_status = self.relay_commit_fr_block(
766                hops,
767                block_index,
768                wait=wait,
769                timeout=timeout,
770                poll_interval=poll_interval,
771            )
772        return last_status
773
774    def relay_wait_fr_write_complete(
775        self,
776        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
777        *,
778        timeout: float = 30.0,
779        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
780    ) -> CpuStatusData:
781        """Poll remote FR flash-write completion status through relay hops."""
782        deadline = time.monotonic() + max(0.0, float(timeout))
783        interval = max(0.01, float(poll_interval))
784        while True:
785            use_a0 = self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0 is not False
786            if use_a0:
787                try:
788                    status = self.relay_read_cpu_status_a0(hops)
789                    self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0 = True
790                except (ToyopucError, ToyopucProtocolError):
791                    err = _extract_response_error_code(self.last_rx)
792                    if err is None:
793                        err = _extract_relay_nak_error_code(self.last_rx)
794                    if err in (0x23, 0x24, 0x25, 0x26):
795                        self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0 = False
796                        status = self.relay_read_cpu_status(hops)
797                    else:
798                        raise
799            else:
800                status = self.relay_read_cpu_status(hops)
801            if status.abnormal_write_flash_register:
802                raise ToyopucError('FR flash write failed: abnormal_write_flash_register=1')
803            if not status.under_writing_flash_register:
804                return status
805            if time.monotonic() >= deadline:
806                raise ToyopucTimeoutError('Timed out waiting for relay FR flash write completion')
807            time.sleep(interval)
808
809    def read_clock(self) -> ClockData:
810        """Read the CPU clock via `CMD=32 / 70 00`."""
811        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_clock_read())
812        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
813            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
814        try:
815            return parse_clock_data(resp.data)
816        except Exception as e:
817            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
818                f'Failed to parse clock response data={resp.data.hex()}'
819            ) from e
820
821    def read_cpu_status(self) -> CpuStatusData:
822        """Read the 8-byte CPU status block via `CMD=32 / 11 00`."""
823        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_cpu_status_read())
824        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
825            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
826        try:
827            return parse_cpu_status_data(resp.data)
828        except Exception as e:
829            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
830                f'Failed to parse CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
831            ) from e
832
833    def read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self) -> bytes:
834        """Read raw 8-byte CPU status via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`.
835
836        This command path is used in the flash/FR completion flow. The library
837        currently returns the 8 raw status bytes because the exact bit mapping
838        for this path has not been finalized yet.
839        """
840        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_cpu_status_read_a0())
841        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
842            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
843        try:
844            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0_raw(resp.data)
845        except Exception as e:
846            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
847                f'Failed to parse A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
848            ) from e
849
850    def read_cpu_status_a0(self) -> CpuStatusData:
851        """Read decoded CPU status via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`."""
852        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_cpu_status_read_a0())
853        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
854            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
855        try:
856            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0(resp.data)
857        except Exception as e:
858            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
859                f'Failed to parse A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
860            ) from e
861
862    def wait_fr_write_complete(self, *, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData:
863        """Poll FR flash-write completion status.
864
865        Prefer `CMD=A0 / 01 10` when available. If the target rejects `A0`
866        with an invalid-subcommand style error, fall back to normal CPU status
867        `CMD=32 / 11 00`, which exposes the same `Data7` flash-write bits on
868        the Nano 10GX tested in this project.
869        """
870        deadline = time.monotonic() + max(0.0, float(timeout))
871        interval = max(0.01, float(poll_interval))
872        while True:
873            use_a0 = self._fr_wait_prefers_a0 is not False
874            if use_a0:
875                try:
876                    status = self.read_cpu_status_a0()
877                    self._fr_wait_prefers_a0 = True
878                except (ToyopucError, ToyopucProtocolError):
879                    err = _extract_response_error_code(self.last_rx)
880                    if err in (0x23, 0x24, 0x25):
881                        self._fr_wait_prefers_a0 = False
882                        status = self.read_cpu_status()
883                    else:
884                        raise
885            else:
886                status = self.read_cpu_status()
887            if status.abnormal_write_flash_register:
888                raise ToyopucError('FR flash write failed: abnormal_write_flash_register=1')
889            if not status.under_writing_flash_register:
890                return status
891            if time.monotonic() >= deadline:
892                raise ToyopucTimeoutError('Timed out waiting for FR flash write completion')
893            time.sleep(interval)
894
895    def write_clock(self, value: datetime) -> None:
896        """Set the CPU clock via `CMD=32 / 71 00`."""
897        weekday = (value.weekday() + 1) % 7  # Python Monday=0, PLC Sunday=0
898        resp = self._send_and_recv(
899            build_clock_write(
900                value.second,
901                value.minute,
902                value.hour,
903                value.day,
904                value.month,
905                value.year % 100,
906                weekday,
907            )
908        )
909        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
910            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
911        if resp.data != bytes([0x71, 0x00]):
912            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected clock write response body')

Low-level TOYOPUC computer-link client.

Use this class when you want explicit control over command families, numeric addresses, and transport settings. For string-address driven use, prefer ToyopucHighLevelClient.

ToyopucClient( host: str, port: int, *, local_port: int = 0, protocol: str = 'tcp', timeout: float = 3.0, retries: int = 0, retry_delay: float = 0.2, recv_bufsize: int = 8192) 
185    def __init__(
186        self,
187        host: str,
188        port: int,
189        *,
190        local_port: int = 0,
191        protocol: str = 'tcp',
192        timeout: float = 3.0,
193        retries: int = 0,
194        retry_delay: float = 0.2,
195        recv_bufsize: int = 8192,
196    ) -> None:
197        self.host = host
198        self.port = port
199        self.local_port = int(local_port)
200        self.protocol = protocol.lower()
201        self.timeout = timeout
202        self.retries = max(0, int(retries))
203        self.retry_delay = float(retry_delay)
204        self.recv_bufsize = recv_bufsize
205        self._sock: Optional[socket.socket] = None
206        self._last_tx: Optional[bytes] = None
207        self._last_rx: Optional[bytes] = None
208        self._fr_wait_prefers_a0: Optional[bool] = None
209        self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0: Optional[bool] = None
host
port
local_port
protocol
timeout
retries
retry_delay
recv_bufsize
def connect(self) -> None: 
218    def connect(self) -> None:
219        if self._sock:
220            return
221        if self.protocol == 'tcp':
222            sock = socket.create_connection((self.host, self.port), self.timeout)
223        elif self.protocol == 'udp':
224            sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
225            if self.local_port:
226                sock.bind(('', self.local_port))
227            sock.settimeout(self.timeout)
228        else:
229            raise ValueError("protocol must be 'tcp' or 'udp'")
230        self._sock = sock
def close(self) -> None: 
232    def close(self) -> None:
233        if self._sock:
234            try:
235                self._sock.close()
236            finally:
237                self._sock = None
238        self._last_tx = None
239        self._last_rx = None
last_tx: bytes | None 
241    @property
242    def last_tx(self) -> Optional[bytes]:
243        return self._last_tx
last_rx: bytes | None 
245    @property
246    def last_rx(self) -> Optional[bytes]:
247        return self._last_rx
def send_raw(self, cmd: int, data: bytes = b'') -> toyopuc.protocol.ResponseFrame: 
323    def send_raw(self, cmd: int, data: bytes = b'') -> ResponseFrame:
324        payload = build_command(cmd, data)
325        return self._send_and_recv(payload)
def send_payload(self, payload: bytes) -> toyopuc.protocol.ResponseFrame: 
327    def send_payload(self, payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
328        """Send a fully-built command payload and return the parsed response."""
329        return self._send_and_recv(payload)

Send a fully-built command payload and return the parsed response.

def read_words(self, addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]: 
331    def read_words(self, addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
332        """Read one or more basic-area words with `CMD=1C`."""
333        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_word_read(addr, count))
334        if resp.cmd != 0x1C:
335            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
336        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)

Read one or more basic-area words with CMD=1C.

def write_words(self, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None: 
338    def write_words(self, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
339        """Write one or more basic-area words with `CMD=1D`."""
340        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_word_write(addr, values))
341        if resp.cmd != 0x1D:
342            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write one or more basic-area words with CMD=1D.

def read_bytes(self, addr: int, count: int) -> bytes: 
344    def read_bytes(self, addr: int, count: int) -> bytes:
345        """Read one or more basic-area bytes with `CMD=1E`."""
346        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_byte_read(addr, count))
347        if resp.cmd != 0x1E:
348            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
349        return resp.data

Read one or more basic-area bytes with CMD=1E.

def write_bytes(self, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None: 
351    def write_bytes(self, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
352        """Write one or more basic-area bytes with `CMD=1F`."""
353        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_byte_write(addr, values))
354        if resp.cmd != 0x1F:
355            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write one or more basic-area bytes with CMD=1F.

def read_bit(self, addr: int) -> bool: 
357    def read_bit(self, addr: int) -> bool:
358        """Read one basic-area bit with `CMD=20`."""
359        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_bit_read(addr))
360        if resp.cmd != 0x20:
361            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
362        if len(resp.data) != 1:
363            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Bit read response must be 1 byte')
364        return resp.data[0] != 0

Read one basic-area bit with CMD=20.

def write_bit(self, addr: int, value: bool) -> None: 
366    def write_bit(self, addr: int, value: bool) -> None:
367        """Write one basic-area bit with `CMD=21`."""
368        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_bit_write(addr, 1 if value else 0))
369        if resp.cmd != 0x21:
370            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write one basic-area bit with CMD=21.

def read_words_multi(self, addrs: Iterable[int]) -> List[int]: 
372    def read_words_multi(self, addrs: Iterable[int]) -> List[int]:
373        """Read multiple non-contiguous basic-area words with `CMD=22`."""
374        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_word_read(addrs))
375        if resp.cmd != 0x22:
376            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
377        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)

Read multiple non-contiguous basic-area words with CMD=22.

def write_words_multi(self, pairs: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> None: 
379    def write_words_multi(self, pairs: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> None:
380        """Write multiple non-contiguous basic-area words with `CMD=23`."""
381        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_word_write(pairs))
382        if resp.cmd != 0x23:
383            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write multiple non-contiguous basic-area words with CMD=23.

def read_bytes_multi(self, addrs: Iterable[int]) -> bytes: 
385    def read_bytes_multi(self, addrs: Iterable[int]) -> bytes:
386        """Read multiple non-contiguous basic-area bytes with `CMD=24`."""
387        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_byte_read(addrs))
388        if resp.cmd != 0x24:
389            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
390        return resp.data

Read multiple non-contiguous basic-area bytes with CMD=24.

def write_bytes_multi(self, pairs: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> None: 
392    def write_bytes_multi(self, pairs: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> None:
393        """Write multiple non-contiguous basic-area bytes with `CMD=25`."""
394        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_multi_byte_write(pairs))
395        if resp.cmd != 0x25:
396            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write multiple non-contiguous basic-area bytes with CMD=25.

def read_ext_words(self, no: int, addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]: 
398    def read_ext_words(self, no: int, addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
399        """Read extended-area words with `CMD=94` using `(No., addr)`."""
400        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_word_read(no, addr, count))
401        if resp.cmd != 0x94:
402            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
403        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)

Read extended-area words with CMD=94 using (No., addr).

def write_ext_words(self, no: int, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None: 
405    def write_ext_words(self, no: int, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
406        """Write extended-area words with `CMD=95` using `(No., addr)`."""
407        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_word_write(no, addr, values))
408        if resp.cmd != 0x95:
409            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write extended-area words with CMD=95 using (No., addr).

def read_ext_bytes(self, no: int, addr: int, count: int) -> bytes: 
411    def read_ext_bytes(self, no: int, addr: int, count: int) -> bytes:
412        """Read extended-area bytes with `CMD=96` using `(No., addr)`."""
413        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_byte_read(no, addr, count))
414        if resp.cmd != 0x96:
415            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
416        return resp.data

Read extended-area bytes with CMD=96 using (No., addr).

def write_ext_bytes(self, no: int, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None: 
418    def write_ext_bytes(self, no: int, addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
419        """Write extended-area bytes with `CMD=97` using `(No., addr)`."""
420        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_ext_byte_write(no, addr, values))
421        if resp.cmd != 0x97:
422            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write extended-area bytes with CMD=97 using (No., addr).

def read_ext_multi( self, bit_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]], byte_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], word_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> bytes: 
424    def read_ext_multi(
425        self,
426        bit_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]],
427        byte_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
428        word_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
429    ) -> bytes:
430        """Read mixed extended points with `CMD=98`.
431
432        `bit_points` items are `(no, bit_no, addr)`.
433        `byte_points` items are `(no, addr)`.
434        `word_points` items are `(no, addr)`.
435        """
436        resp = self._send_and_recv(
437            build_ext_multi_read(list(bit_points), list(byte_points), list(word_points))
438        )
439        if resp.cmd != 0x98:
440            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
441        return resp.data

Read mixed extended points with CMD=98.

bit_points items are (no, bit_no, addr). byte_points items are (no, addr). word_points items are (no, addr).

def write_ext_multi( self, bit_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int, int]], byte_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]], word_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]]) -> None: 
443    def write_ext_multi(
444        self,
445        bit_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int, int]],
446        byte_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]],
447        word_points: Iterable[Tuple[int, int, int]],
448    ) -> None:
449        """Write mixed extended points with `CMD=99`."""
450        resp = self._send_and_recv(
451            build_ext_multi_write(list(bit_points), list(byte_points), list(word_points))
452        )
453        if resp.cmd != 0x99:
454            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write mixed extended points with CMD=99.

def pc10_block_read(self, addr32: int, count: int) -> bytes: 
456    def pc10_block_read(self, addr32: int, count: int) -> bytes:
457        """Read PC10 block data with `CMD=C2` from a 32-bit byte address."""
458        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_block_read(addr32, count))
459        if resp.cmd != 0xC2:
460            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
461        return resp.data

Read PC10 block data with CMD=C2 from a 32-bit byte address.

def pc10_block_write(self, addr32: int, data_bytes: bytes) -> None: 
463    def pc10_block_write(self, addr32: int, data_bytes: bytes) -> None:
464        """Write PC10 block data with `CMD=C3` to a 32-bit byte address."""
465        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_block_write(addr32, data_bytes))
466        if resp.cmd != 0xC3:
467            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write PC10 block data with CMD=C3 to a 32-bit byte address.

def pc10_multi_read(self, payload: bytes) -> bytes: 
469    def pc10_multi_read(self, payload: bytes) -> bytes:
470        """Read PC10 multi-point data with `CMD=C4` using a prebuilt payload."""
471        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_multi_read(payload))
472        if resp.cmd != 0xC4:
473            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
474        return resp.data

Read PC10 multi-point data with CMD=C4 using a prebuilt payload.

def pc10_multi_write(self, payload: bytes) -> None: 
476    def pc10_multi_write(self, payload: bytes) -> None:
477        """Write PC10 multi-point data with `CMD=C5` using a prebuilt payload."""
478        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_pc10_multi_write(payload))
479        if resp.cmd != 0xC5:
480            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Write PC10 multi-point data with CMD=C5 using a prebuilt payload.

def read_fr_words(self, index: int, count: int) -> List[int]: 
482    def read_fr_words(self, index: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
483        """Read FR words via PC10 block read (`CMD=C2`).
484
485        `FR` real-hardware access uses 32-bit PC10 addressing with
486        `Ex No.=0x40-0x7F`, not `CMD=94`.
487        """
488        values: List[int] = []
489        for chunk_index, chunk_words in _iter_fr_io_segments(index, count):
490            data = self.pc10_block_read(encode_fr_word_addr32(chunk_index), chunk_words * 2)
491            values.extend(unpack_u16_le(data))
492        return values

Read FR words via PC10 block read (CMD=C2).

FR real-hardware access uses 32-bit PC10 addressing with Ex No.=0x40-0x7F, not CMD=94.

def write_fr_words(self, index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False) -> None: 
494    def write_fr_words(self, index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False) -> None:
495        """Write FR words via PC10 block write (`CMD=C3`).
496
497        This updates the FR work area. Persisting the block to flash requires
498        `CMD=CA`. Set `commit=True` to commit every affected FR block after the
499        write completes.
500        """
501        self.write_fr_words_ex(index, values, commit=commit, wait=commit)

Write FR words via PC10 block write (CMD=C3).

This updates the FR work area. Persisting the block to flash requires CMD=CA. Set commit=True to commit every affected FR block after the write completes.

def write_fr_words_ex( self, index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> None: 
503    def write_fr_words_ex(
504        self,
505        index: int,
506        values: Iterable[int],
507        *,
508        commit: bool = False,
509        wait: bool = False,
510        timeout: float = 30.0,
511        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
512    ) -> None:
513        """Write FR words, with optional commit and completion wait.
514
515        When ``commit=True``, this follows the manual's 64-kbyte block unit:
516        each affected FR block is written with `C3`, committed with `CA`, and
517        optionally waited on before moving to the next block.
518        """
519        vals = [int(v) & 0xFFFF for v in values]
520        if not vals:
521            raise ValueError('values must contain at least one word')
522        offset = 0
523        for block_index, block_words in _iter_fr_segments(index, len(vals)):
524            block_offset = 0
525            while block_offset < block_words:
526                chunk_words = min(block_words - block_offset, _FR_IO_CHUNK_WORDS)
527                chunk_vals = vals[offset : offset + chunk_words]
528                data = b''.join(v.to_bytes(2, 'little') for v in chunk_vals)
529                self.pc10_block_write(encode_fr_word_addr32(block_index + block_offset), data)
530                offset += chunk_words
531                block_offset += chunk_words
532            if commit:
533                self.commit_fr_block(
534                    block_index,
535                    wait=wait,
536                    timeout=timeout,
537                    poll_interval=poll_interval,
538                )

Write FR words, with optional commit and completion wait.

When commit=True, this follows the manual's 64-kbyte block unit: each affected FR block is written with C3, committed with CA, and optionally waited on before moving to the next block.

def commit_fr_block( self, index: int, *, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData | None: 
540    def commit_fr_block(
541        self,
542        index: int,
543        *,
544        wait: bool = False,
545        timeout: float = 30.0,
546        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
547    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
548        """Commit the FR block containing `index` via `CMD=CA`.
549
550        By default this waits until `Data7.bit4` (`under_writing_flash_register`)
551        clears and raises on `Data7.bit5` (`abnormal_write_flash_register`).
552        """
553        self.fr_register(fr_block_ex_no(index))
554        if wait:
555            return self.wait_fr_write_complete(timeout=timeout, poll_interval=poll_interval)
556        return None

Commit the FR block containing index via CMD=CA.

By default this waits until Data7.bit4 (under_writing_flash_register) clears and raises on Data7.bit5 (abnormal_write_flash_register).

def commit_fr_range( self, index: int, count: int = 1, *, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData | None: 
558    def commit_fr_range(
559        self,
560        index: int,
561        count: int = 1,
562        *,
563        wait: bool = False,
564        timeout: float = 30.0,
565        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
566    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
567        """Commit every FR block touched by a contiguous word range."""
568        last_status: Optional[CpuStatusData] = None
569        for block_index in _fr_commit_blocks(index, count):
570            last_status = self.commit_fr_block(
571                block_index,
572                wait=wait,
573                timeout=timeout,
574                poll_interval=poll_interval,
575            )
576        return last_status

Commit every FR block touched by a contiguous word range.

def write_fr_words_committed(self, index: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None: 
578    def write_fr_words_committed(self, index: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
579        """Write FR words and commit every affected FR block."""
580        self.write_fr_words_ex(index, values, commit=True, wait=True)

Write FR words and commit every affected FR block.

def fr_register(self, ex_no: int) -> None: 
582    def fr_register(self, ex_no: int) -> None:
583        """Issue the FR-register command `CMD=CA`."""
584        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_fr_register(ex_no))
585        if resp.cmd != 0xCA:
586            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')

Issue the FR-register command CMD=CA.

def relay_command( self, link_no: int, station_no: int, inner_payload: bytes) -> toyopuc.protocol.ResponseFrame: 
588    def relay_command(self, link_no: int, station_no: int, inner_payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
589        """Wrap a command in one relay hop using `CMD=60`."""
590        return self._send_and_recv(build_relay_command(link_no, station_no, inner_payload))

Wrap a command in one relay hop using CMD=60.

def relay_nested( self, hops: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], inner_payload: bytes) -> toyopuc.protocol.ResponseFrame: 
592    def relay_nested(self, hops: Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], inner_payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
593        """Wrap a command in multiple relay hops using nested `CMD=60` frames."""
594        return self._send_and_recv(build_relay_nested(list(hops), inner_payload))

Wrap a command in multiple relay hops using nested CMD=60 frames.

def send_via_relay( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], inner_payload: bytes) -> toyopuc.protocol.ResponseFrame: 
596    def send_via_relay(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], inner_payload: bytes) -> ResponseFrame:
597        """Send a command through relay hops and return the final inner response."""
598        outer = self.relay_nested(normalize_relay_hops(hops), inner_payload)
599        layers, final = unwrap_relay_response_chain(outer)
600        if final is None:
601            last = layers[-1]
602            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
603                f"Relay NAK at link=0x{last.link_no:02X}, station=0x{last.station_no:04X}, ack=0x{last.ack:02X}"
604            )
605        return final

Send a command through relay hops and return the final inner response.

def relay_read_words( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]: 
607    def relay_read_words(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], addr: int, count: int) -> List[int]:
608        """Read one or more basic-area words through relay hops."""
609        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_word_read(addr, count))
610        if resp.cmd != 0x1C:
611            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay word-read response')
612        return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)

Read one or more basic-area words through relay hops.

def relay_write_words( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None: 
614    def relay_write_words(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], addr: int, values: Iterable[int]) -> None:
615        """Write one or more basic-area words through relay hops."""
616        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_word_write(addr, values))
617        if resp.cmd != 0x1D:
618            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay word-write response')

Write one or more basic-area words through relay hops.

def relay_read_clock( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> ClockData: 
620    def relay_read_clock(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> ClockData:
621        """Read the CPU clock through relay hops."""
622        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_clock_read())
623        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
624            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay clock response')
625        try:
626            return parse_clock_data(resp.data)
627        except Exception as e:
628            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
629                f'Failed to parse relay clock response data={resp.data.hex()}'
630            ) from e

Read the CPU clock through relay hops.

def relay_write_clock( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], value: datetime.datetime) -> None: 
632    def relay_write_clock(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], value: datetime) -> None:
633        """Set the CPU clock through relay hops via `CMD=32 / 71 00`."""
634        weekday = (value.weekday() + 1) % 7
635        resp = self.send_via_relay(
636            hops,
637            build_clock_write(
638                value.second,
639                value.minute,
640                value.hour,
641                value.day,
642                value.month,
643                value.year % 100,
644                weekday,
645            ),
646        )
647        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
648            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay clock-write response')
649        if resp.data != bytes([0x71, 0x00]):
650            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected relay clock-write response body')

Set the CPU clock through relay hops via CMD=32 / 71 00.

def relay_read_cpu_status( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> CpuStatusData: 
652    def relay_read_cpu_status(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> CpuStatusData:
653        """Read the 8-byte CPU status block through relay hops."""
654        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_cpu_status_read())
655        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
656            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay CPU status response')
657        try:
658            return parse_cpu_status_data(resp.data)
659        except Exception as e:
660            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
661                f'Failed to parse relay CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
662            ) from e

Read the 8-byte CPU status block through relay hops.

def relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> bytes: 
664    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> bytes:
665        """Read raw 8-byte CPU status through relay hops via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`."""
666        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_cpu_status_read_a0())
667        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
668            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay A0 CPU status response')
669        try:
670            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0_raw(resp.data)
671        except Exception as e:
672            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
673                f'Failed to parse relay A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
674            ) from e

Read raw 8-byte CPU status through relay hops via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

def relay_read_cpu_status_a0( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> CpuStatusData: 
676    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> CpuStatusData:
677        """Read decoded CPU status through relay hops via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`."""
678        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_cpu_status_read_a0())
679        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
680            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay A0 CPU status response')
681        try:
682            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0(resp.data)
683        except Exception as e:
684            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
685                f'Failed to parse relay A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
686            ) from e

Read decoded CPU status through relay hops via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

def relay_write_fr_words( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False) -> None: 
688    def relay_write_fr_words(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False) -> None:
689        """Write FR words through relay hops, optionally committing touched blocks."""
690        self.relay_write_fr_words_ex(hops, index, values, commit=commit, wait=commit)

Write FR words through relay hops, optionally committing touched blocks.

def relay_write_fr_words_ex( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], index: int, values: Iterable[int], *, commit: bool = False, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> None: 
692    def relay_write_fr_words_ex(
693        self,
694        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
695        index: int,
696        values: Iterable[int],
697        *,
698        commit: bool = False,
699        wait: bool = False,
700        timeout: float = 30.0,
701        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
702    ) -> None:
703        """Write FR words through relay hops, with optional commit and completion wait."""
704        vals = [int(v) & 0xFFFF for v in values]
705        if not vals:
706            raise ValueError('values must contain at least one word')
707        offset = 0
708        for block_index, block_words in _iter_fr_segments(index, len(vals)):
709            block_offset = 0
710            while block_offset < block_words:
711                chunk_words = min(block_words - block_offset, _FR_IO_CHUNK_WORDS)
712                chunk_vals = vals[offset : offset + chunk_words]
713                data = b''.join(v.to_bytes(2, 'little') for v in chunk_vals)
714                resp = self.send_via_relay(
715                    hops,
716                    build_pc10_block_write(encode_fr_word_addr32(block_index + block_offset), data),
717                )
718                if resp.cmd != 0xC3:
719                    raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay FR block-write response')
720                offset += chunk_words
721                block_offset += chunk_words
722            if commit:
723                self.relay_commit_fr_block(
724                    hops,
725                    block_index,
726                    wait=wait,
727                    timeout=timeout,
728                    poll_interval=poll_interval,
729                )

Write FR words through relay hops, with optional commit and completion wait.

def relay_fr_register(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], ex_no: int) -> None: 
731    def relay_fr_register(self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], ex_no: int) -> None:
732        """Issue the FR-register command `CMD=CA` through relay hops."""
733        resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_fr_register(ex_no))
734        if resp.cmd != 0xCA:
735            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in relay FR-register response')

Issue the FR-register command CMD=CA through relay hops.

def relay_commit_fr_block( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], index: int, *, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData | None: 
737    def relay_commit_fr_block(
738        self,
739        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
740        index: int,
741        *,
742        wait: bool = False,
743        timeout: float = 30.0,
744        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
745    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
746        """Commit the remote FR block containing `index` via relay `CMD=CA`."""
747        self.relay_fr_register(hops, fr_block_ex_no(index))
748        if wait:
749            return self.relay_wait_fr_write_complete(hops, timeout=timeout, poll_interval=poll_interval)
750        return None

Commit the remote FR block containing index via relay CMD=CA.

def relay_commit_fr_range( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], index: int, count: int = 1, *, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData | None: 
752    def relay_commit_fr_range(
753        self,
754        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
755        index: int,
756        count: int = 1,
757        *,
758        wait: bool = False,
759        timeout: float = 30.0,
760        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
761    ) -> Optional[CpuStatusData]:
762        """Commit every FR block touched by a contiguous relay FR range."""
763        last_status: Optional[CpuStatusData] = None
764        for block_index in _fr_commit_blocks(index, count):
765            last_status = self.relay_commit_fr_block(
766                hops,
767                block_index,
768                wait=wait,
769                timeout=timeout,
770                poll_interval=poll_interval,
771            )
772        return last_status

Commit every FR block touched by a contiguous relay FR range.

def relay_wait_fr_write_complete( self, hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]], *, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData: 
774    def relay_wait_fr_write_complete(
775        self,
776        hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]],
777        *,
778        timeout: float = 30.0,
779        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
780    ) -> CpuStatusData:
781        """Poll remote FR flash-write completion status through relay hops."""
782        deadline = time.monotonic() + max(0.0, float(timeout))
783        interval = max(0.01, float(poll_interval))
784        while True:
785            use_a0 = self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0 is not False
786            if use_a0:
787                try:
788                    status = self.relay_read_cpu_status_a0(hops)
789                    self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0 = True
790                except (ToyopucError, ToyopucProtocolError):
791                    err = _extract_response_error_code(self.last_rx)
792                    if err is None:
793                        err = _extract_relay_nak_error_code(self.last_rx)
794                    if err in (0x23, 0x24, 0x25, 0x26):
795                        self._relay_fr_wait_prefers_a0 = False
796                        status = self.relay_read_cpu_status(hops)
797                    else:
798                        raise
799            else:
800                status = self.relay_read_cpu_status(hops)
801            if status.abnormal_write_flash_register:
802                raise ToyopucError('FR flash write failed: abnormal_write_flash_register=1')
803            if not status.under_writing_flash_register:
804                return status
805            if time.monotonic() >= deadline:
806                raise ToyopucTimeoutError('Timed out waiting for relay FR flash write completion')
807            time.sleep(interval)

Poll remote FR flash-write completion status through relay hops.

def read_clock(self) -> ClockData: 
809    def read_clock(self) -> ClockData:
810        """Read the CPU clock via `CMD=32 / 70 00`."""
811        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_clock_read())
812        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
813            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
814        try:
815            return parse_clock_data(resp.data)
816        except Exception as e:
817            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
818                f'Failed to parse clock response data={resp.data.hex()}'
819            ) from e

Read the CPU clock via CMD=32 / 70 00.

def read_cpu_status(self) -> CpuStatusData: 
821    def read_cpu_status(self) -> CpuStatusData:
822        """Read the 8-byte CPU status block via `CMD=32 / 11 00`."""
823        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_cpu_status_read())
824        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
825            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
826        try:
827            return parse_cpu_status_data(resp.data)
828        except Exception as e:
829            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
830                f'Failed to parse CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
831            ) from e

Read the 8-byte CPU status block via CMD=32 / 11 00.

def read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self) -> bytes: 
833    def read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self) -> bytes:
834        """Read raw 8-byte CPU status via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`.
835
836        This command path is used in the flash/FR completion flow. The library
837        currently returns the 8 raw status bytes because the exact bit mapping
838        for this path has not been finalized yet.
839        """
840        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_cpu_status_read_a0())
841        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
842            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
843        try:
844            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0_raw(resp.data)
845        except Exception as e:
846            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
847                f'Failed to parse A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
848            ) from e

Read raw 8-byte CPU status via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

This command path is used in the flash/FR completion flow. The library currently returns the 8 raw status bytes because the exact bit mapping for this path has not been finalized yet.

def read_cpu_status_a0(self) -> CpuStatusData: 
850    def read_cpu_status_a0(self) -> CpuStatusData:
851        """Read decoded CPU status via `CMD=A0 / 01 10`."""
852        resp = self._send_and_recv(build_cpu_status_read_a0())
853        if resp.cmd != 0xA0:
854            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
855        try:
856            return parse_cpu_status_data_a0(resp.data)
857        except Exception as e:
858            raise ToyopucProtocolError(
859                f'Failed to parse A0 CPU status response data={resp.data.hex()}'
860            ) from e

Read decoded CPU status via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

def wait_fr_write_complete( self, *, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData: 
862    def wait_fr_write_complete(self, *, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> CpuStatusData:
863        """Poll FR flash-write completion status.
864
865        Prefer `CMD=A0 / 01 10` when available. If the target rejects `A0`
866        with an invalid-subcommand style error, fall back to normal CPU status
867        `CMD=32 / 11 00`, which exposes the same `Data7` flash-write bits on
868        the Nano 10GX tested in this project.
869        """
870        deadline = time.monotonic() + max(0.0, float(timeout))
871        interval = max(0.01, float(poll_interval))
872        while True:
873            use_a0 = self._fr_wait_prefers_a0 is not False
874            if use_a0:
875                try:
876                    status = self.read_cpu_status_a0()
877                    self._fr_wait_prefers_a0 = True
878                except (ToyopucError, ToyopucProtocolError):
879                    err = _extract_response_error_code(self.last_rx)
880                    if err in (0x23, 0x24, 0x25):
881                        self._fr_wait_prefers_a0 = False
882                        status = self.read_cpu_status()
883                    else:
884                        raise
885            else:
886                status = self.read_cpu_status()
887            if status.abnormal_write_flash_register:
888                raise ToyopucError('FR flash write failed: abnormal_write_flash_register=1')
889            if not status.under_writing_flash_register:
890                return status
891            if time.monotonic() >= deadline:
892                raise ToyopucTimeoutError('Timed out waiting for FR flash write completion')
893            time.sleep(interval)

Poll FR flash-write completion status.

Prefer CMD=A0 / 01 10 when available. If the target rejects A0 with an invalid-subcommand style error, fall back to normal CPU status CMD=32 / 11 00, which exposes the same Data7 flash-write bits on the Nano 10GX tested in this project.

def write_clock(self, value: datetime.datetime) -> None: 
895    def write_clock(self, value: datetime) -> None:
896        """Set the CPU clock via `CMD=32 / 71 00`."""
897        weekday = (value.weekday() + 1) % 7  # Python Monday=0, PLC Sunday=0
898        resp = self._send_and_recv(
899            build_clock_write(
900                value.second,
901                value.minute,
902                value.hour,
903                value.day,
904                value.month,
905                value.year % 100,
906                weekday,
907            )
908        )
909        if resp.cmd != 0x32:
910            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected CMD in response')
911        if resp.data != bytes([0x71, 0x00]):
912            raise ToyopucProtocolError('Unexpected clock write response body')

Set the CPU clock via CMD=32 / 71 00.

class ToyopucHighLevelClient(toyopuc.ToyopucClient): 
 441class ToyopucHighLevelClient(ToyopucClient):
 442    """High-level client that accepts string device addresses.
 443
 444    This wrapper hides branching between basic, prefixed, extended, and PC10
 445    access paths. It is the default client for application code.
 446
 447    Use this class when you want the library to choose the correct low-level
 448    command family from a string such as ``D0000``, ``P1-M0000``, ``EX0010W``,
 449    or ``GX0010H``.
 450    """
 451
 452    def read_cpu_status(self):
 453        return super().read_cpu_status()
 454
 455    def relay_read_cpu_status(self, hops):
 456        return super().relay_read_cpu_status(hops)
 457
 458    def read_cpu_status_a0(self):
 459        return super().read_cpu_status_a0()
 460
 461    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0(self, hops):
 462        return super().relay_read_cpu_status_a0(hops)
 463
 464    def read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self):
 465        return super().read_cpu_status_a0_raw()
 466
 467    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self, hops):
 468        return super().relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(hops)
 469
 470    def read_clock(self):
 471        return super().read_clock()
 472
 473    def relay_read_clock(self, hops):
 474        return super().relay_read_clock(hops)
 475
 476    def relay_write_clock(self, hops, value: datetime) -> None:
 477        super().relay_write_clock(hops, value)
 478
 479    def write_clock(self, value: datetime) -> None:
 480        super().write_clock(value)
 481
 482    def resolve_device(self, device: str) -> ResolvedDevice:
 483        """Resolve a string address into a `ResolvedDevice`."""
 484        return resolve_device(device)
 485
 486    def relay_read(self, hops, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
 487        """Read one item or a contiguous sequence through relay hops."""
 488        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 489        if count < 1:
 490            raise ValueError("count must be >= 1")
 491        if count == 1:
 492            return self._relay_read_one(hops, resolved)
 493        return [self._relay_read_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i)) for i in range(count)]
 494
 495    def relay_write(self, hops, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], value) -> None:
 496        """Write one item or a contiguous sequence through relay hops."""
 497        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 498        if resolved.unit == "bit":
 499            if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
 500                for i, item in enumerate(value):
 501                    self._relay_write_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i), item)
 502                return
 503            self._relay_write_one(hops, resolved, value)
 504            return
 505
 506        if isinstance(value, (bytes, bytearray)):
 507            for i, item in enumerate(value):
 508                self._relay_write_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i), item)
 509            return
 510        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
 511            for i, item in enumerate(value):
 512                self._relay_write_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i), item)
 513            return
 514        self._relay_write_one(hops, resolved, value)
 515
 516    def relay_read_words(
 517        self,
 518        hops: str | Iterable[tuple[int, int]],
 519        device: Union[int, str, ResolvedDevice],
 520        count: int = 1,
 521    ) -> List[int]:
 522        """Read one or more word devices through relay hops."""
 523        if isinstance(device, int):
 524            return super().relay_read_words(hops, device, count)
 525        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 526        if resolved.unit != "word":
 527            raise ValueError("relay_read_words() requires a word device")
 528        values = self.relay_read(hops, resolved, count)
 529        if isinstance(values, list):
 530            return [int(item) for item in values]
 531        return [int(values)]
 532
 533    def relay_write_words(
 534        self,
 535        hops: str | Iterable[tuple[int, int]],
 536        device: Union[int, str, ResolvedDevice],
 537        value: Union[Iterable[int], int],
 538    ) -> None:
 539        """Write one or more word devices through relay hops."""
 540        if isinstance(device, int):
 541            if isinstance(value, int):
 542                super().relay_write_words(hops, device, [value])
 543            else:
 544                super().relay_write_words(hops, device, value)
 545            return
 546        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 547        if resolved.unit != "word":
 548            raise ValueError("relay_write_words() requires a word device")
 549        self.relay_write(hops, resolved, value)
 550
 551    def relay_read_many(self, hops, devices: Sequence[Union[str, ResolvedDevice]]) -> List[object]:
 552        """Read multiple devices through relay hops and preserve input order."""
 553        return [self.relay_read(hops, device) for device in devices]
 554
 555    def relay_write_many(self, hops, items: Mapping[Union[str, ResolvedDevice], object]) -> None:
 556        """Write multiple devices through relay hops in input order."""
 557        for device, value in items.items():
 558            self.relay_write(hops, device, value)
 559
 560    def read_fr(self, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
 561        """Read one or more FR words using the dedicated FR path."""
 562        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 563        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
 564            raise ValueError("read_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
 565        values = self.read_fr_words(resolved.index, count)
 566        return values[0] if count == 1 else values
 567
 568    def relay_read_fr(self, hops, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
 569        """Read one or more FR words through relay hops."""
 570        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 571        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
 572            raise ValueError("relay_read_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
 573        return self.relay_read(hops, resolved, count)
 574
 575    def write_fr(
