 576        self,
 577        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
 578        value,
 579        *,
 580        commit: bool = False,
 581        wait: Optional[bool] = None,
 582        timeout: float = 30.0,
 583        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
 584    ) -> None:
 585        """Write one or more FR words, optionally committing and waiting."""
 586        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 587        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
 588            raise ValueError("write_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
 589        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
 590            values = [int(item) for item in value]
 591        else:
 592            values = [int(value)]
 593        should_wait = bool(commit) if wait is None else bool(wait)
 594        self.write_fr_words_ex(
 595            resolved.index,
 596            values,
 597            commit=commit,
 598            wait=should_wait,
 599            timeout=timeout,
 600            poll_interval=poll_interval,
 601        )
 602
 603    def relay_write_fr(
 604        self,
 605        hops,
 606        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
 607        value,
 608        *,
 609        commit: bool = False,
 610        wait: Optional[bool] = None,
 611        timeout: float = 30.0,
 612        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
 613    ) -> None:
 614        """Write one or more FR words through relay hops, optionally committing."""
 615        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 616        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
 617            raise ValueError("relay_write_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
 618        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
 619            values = [int(item) for item in value]
 620        else:
 621            values = [int(value)]
 622        should_wait = bool(commit) if wait is None else bool(wait)
 623        self.relay_write_fr_words_ex(
 624            hops,
 625            resolved.index,
 626            values,
 627            commit=commit,
 628            wait=should_wait,
 629            timeout=timeout,
 630            poll_interval=poll_interval,
 631        )
 632
 633    def commit_fr(
 634        self,
 635        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
 636        count: int = 1,
 637        *,
 638        wait: bool = False,
 639        timeout: float = 30.0,
 640        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
 641    ) -> None:
 642        """Commit every FR block touched by the given FR word range."""
 643        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 644        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
 645            raise ValueError("commit_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
 646        self.commit_fr_range(
 647            resolved.index,
 648            count,
 649            wait=wait,
 650            timeout=timeout,
 651            poll_interval=poll_interval,
 652        )
 653
 654    def relay_commit_fr(
 655        self,
 656        hops,
 657        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
 658        count: int = 1,
 659        *,
 660        wait: bool = False,
 661        timeout: float = 30.0,
 662        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
 663    ) -> None:
 664        """Commit every FR block touched by the given FR word range through relay hops."""
 665        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 666        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
 667            raise ValueError("relay_commit_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
 668        self.relay_commit_fr_range(
 669            hops,
 670            resolved.index,
 671            count,
 672            wait=wait,
 673            timeout=timeout,
 674            poll_interval=poll_interval,
 675        )
 676
 677    def read(self, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
 678        """Read one item or a contiguous sequence from a device address.
 679
 680        Args:
 681            device: String address or previously resolved device descriptor.
 682            count: Number of contiguous items to read.
 683
 684        Returns:
 685            A scalar when ``count == 1``. Otherwise a list of values in device
 686            order.
 687        """
 688        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 689        if count < 1:
 690            raise ValueError("count must be >= 1")
 691        if count == 1:
 692            return self._read_one(resolved)
 693        return [self._read_one(self._offset(resolved, i)) for i in range(count)]
 694
 695    def write(self, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], value) -> None:
 696        """Write one item or a contiguous sequence to a device address.
 697
 698        Args:
 699            device: String address or previously resolved device descriptor.
 700            value: Scalar, sequence, or bytes-like object depending on the
 701                target unit.
 702        """
 703        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 704        if resolved.area == "FR":
 705            _raise_generic_fr_write_error()
 706        if resolved.unit == "bit":
 707            if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
 708                for i, item in enumerate(value):
 709                    self._write_one(self._offset(resolved, i), item)
 710                return
 711            self._write_one(resolved, value)
 712            return
 713
 714        if isinstance(value, (bytes, bytearray)):
 715            for i, item in enumerate(value):
 716                self._write_one(self._offset(resolved, i), item)
 717            return
 718        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
 719            for i, item in enumerate(value):
 720                self._write_one(self._offset(resolved, i), item)
 721            return
 722        self._write_one(resolved, value)
 723
 724    def read_many(self, devices: Sequence[Union[str, ResolvedDevice]]) -> List[object]:
 725        """Read multiple devices and preserve input order.
 726
 727        Args:
 728            devices: Sequence of string addresses or resolved device objects.
 729
 730        Returns:
 731            List of values in the same order as ``devices``.
 732
 733        Notes:
 734            This method currently favors simple, predictable dispatch over
 735            grouped transport optimization.
 736        """
 737        resolved = [self.resolve_device(d) if isinstance(d, str) else d for d in devices]
 738        return [self._read_one(item) for item in resolved]
 739
 740    def write_many(self, items: Mapping[Union[str, ResolvedDevice], object]) -> None:
 741        """Write multiple devices in mapping iteration order.
 742
 743        Args:
 744            items: Mapping of device -> value pairs.
 745
 746        Notes:
 747            This method currently dispatches each item independently.
 748        """
 749        resolved_items = []
 750        for device, value in items.items():
 751            resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
 752            if resolved.area == "FR":
 753                _raise_generic_fr_write_error()
 754            resolved_items.append((resolved, value))
 755        for resolved, value in resolved_items:
 756            self._write_one(resolved, value)
 757
 758    def _read_one(self, resolved: ResolvedDevice):
 759        if resolved.scheme == "basic-bit":
 760            addr = _require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr")
 761            return self.read_bit(addr)
 762        if resolved.scheme == "basic-word":
 763            addr = _require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr")
 764            return self.read_words(addr, 1)[0]
 765        if resolved.scheme == "basic-byte":
 766            addr = _require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr")
 767            return self.read_bytes(addr, 1)[0]
 768        if resolved.scheme == "program-bit":
 769            no = _require(resolved.no, "program number")
 770            bit_no = _require(resolved.bit_no, "program bit")
 771            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "program addr")
 772            return bool(self.read_ext_multi([(no, bit_no, addr)], [], [])[0] & 0x01)
 773        if resolved.scheme == "program-word":
 774            no = _require(resolved.no, "program number")
 775            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "program addr")
 776            return self.read_ext_words(no, addr, 1)[0]
 777        if resolved.scheme == "program-byte":
 778            no = _require(resolved.no, "program number")
 779            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "program addr")
 780            return self.read_ext_bytes(no, addr, 1)[0]
 781        if resolved.scheme == "ext-bit":
 782            no = _require(resolved.no, "extended number")
 783            bit_no = _require(resolved.bit_no, "extended bit")
 784            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr")
 785            return bool(self.read_ext_multi([(no, bit_no, addr)], [], [])[0] & 0x01)
 786        if resolved.scheme == "ext-word":
 787            no = _require(resolved.no, "extended number")
 788            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr")
 789            return self.read_ext_words(no, addr, 1)[0]
 790        if resolved.scheme == "ext-byte":
 791            no = _require(resolved.no, "extended number")
 792            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr")
 793            return self.read_ext_bytes(no, addr, 1)[0]
 794        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-bit":
 795            addr32 = _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32")
 796            return bool(_read_pc10_multi_bits(self, [addr32])[0])
 797        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-word":
 798            addr32 = _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32")
 799            return _read_pc10_block_word(self, addr32)
 800        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-byte":
 801            addr32 = _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32")
 802            return self.pc10_block_read(addr32, 1)[0]
 803        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported resolved scheme: {resolved.scheme}")
 804
 805    def _relay_read_one(self, hops, resolved: ResolvedDevice):
 806        if resolved.scheme == "basic-bit":
 807            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_bit_read(_require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr")))
 808            if resp.cmd != 0x20:
 809                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay bit-read response")
 810            if len(resp.data) != 1:
 811                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay bit-read response must be 1 byte")
 812            return bool(resp.data[0] & 0x01)
 813        if resolved.scheme == "basic-word":
 814            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_word_read(_require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr"), 1))
 815            if resp.cmd != 0x1C:
 816                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay word-read response")
 817            return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)[0]
 818        if resolved.scheme == "basic-byte":
 819            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_byte_read(_require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr"), 1))
 820            if resp.cmd != 0x1E:
 821                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay byte-read response")
 822            if len(resp.data) != 1:
 823                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay byte-read response must be 1 byte")
 824            return resp.data[0]
 825        if resolved.scheme == "program-bit":
 826            resp = self.send_via_relay(
 827                hops,
 828                build_ext_multi_read(
 829                    [(_require(resolved.no, "program number"), _require(resolved.bit_no, "program bit"), _require(resolved.addr, "program addr"))],
 830                    [],
 831                    [],
 832                ),
 833            )
 834            if resp.cmd != 0x98:
 835                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay multi-read response")
 836            if not resp.data:
 837                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay multi-read response missing bit payload")
 838            return bool(resp.data[0] & 0x01)
 839        if resolved.scheme == "program-word":
 840            resp = self.send_via_relay(
 841                hops,
 842                build_ext_word_read(_require(resolved.no, "program number"), _require(resolved.addr, "program addr"), 1),
 843            )
 844            if resp.cmd != 0x94:
 845                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext word-read response")
 846            return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)[0]
 847        if resolved.scheme == "program-byte":
 848            resp = self.send_via_relay(
 849                hops,
 850                build_ext_byte_read(_require(resolved.no, "program number"), _require(resolved.addr, "program addr"), 1),
 851            )
 852            if resp.cmd != 0x96:
 853                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext byte-read response")
 854            if len(resp.data) != 1:
 855                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay ext byte-read response must be 1 byte")
 856            return resp.data[0]
 857        if resolved.scheme == "ext-bit":
 858            resp = self.send_via_relay(
 859                hops,
 860                build_ext_multi_read(
 861                    [(_require(resolved.no, "extended number"), _require(resolved.bit_no, "extended bit"), _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr"))],
 862                    [],
 863                    [],
 864                ),
 865            )
 866            if resp.cmd != 0x98:
 867                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay multi-read response")
 868            if not resp.data:
 869                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay multi-read response missing bit payload")
 870            return bool(resp.data[0] & 0x01)
 871        if resolved.scheme == "ext-word":
 872            resp = self.send_via_relay(
 873                hops,
 874                build_ext_word_read(_require(resolved.no, "extended number"), _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr"), 1),
 875            )
 876            if resp.cmd != 0x94:
 877                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext word-read response")
 878            return unpack_u16_le(resp.data)[0]
 879        if resolved.scheme == "ext-byte":
 880            resp = self.send_via_relay(
 881                hops,
 882                build_ext_byte_read(_require(resolved.no, "extended number"), _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr"), 1),
 883            )
 884            if resp.cmd != 0x96:
 885                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext byte-read response")
 886            if len(resp.data) != 1:
 887                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay ext byte-read response must be 1 byte")
 888            return resp.data[0]
 889        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-bit":
 890            addr32 = _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32")
 891            payload = bytearray([0x01, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00])
 892            payload.extend(addr32.to_bytes(4, "little"))
 893            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_pc10_multi_read(bytes(payload)))
 894            if resp.cmd != 0xC4:
 895                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay PC10 multi-read response")
 896            if len(resp.data) < 5:
 897                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay PC10 bit-read response too short")
 898            return bool(resp.data[4] & 0x01)
 899        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-word":
 900            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_pc10_block_read(_require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32"), 2))
 901            if resp.cmd != 0xC2:
 902                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay PC10 block-read response")
 903            if len(resp.data) < 2:
 904                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay PC10 word-read response too short")
 905            return int.from_bytes(resp.data[:2], "little")
 906        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-byte":
 907            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_pc10_block_read(_require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32"), 1))
 908            if resp.cmd != 0xC2:
 909                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay PC10 block-read response")
 910            if len(resp.data) < 1:
 911                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Relay PC10 byte-read response too short")
 912            return resp.data[0]
 913        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported resolved scheme: {resolved.scheme}")
 914
 915    def _write_one(self, resolved: ResolvedDevice, value) -> None:
 916        if resolved.area == "FR":
 917            _raise_generic_fr_write_error()
 918        if resolved.scheme == "basic-bit":
 919            addr = _require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr")
 920            self.write_bit(addr, bool(value))
 921            return
 922        if resolved.scheme == "basic-word":
 923            addr = _require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr")
 924            self.write_words(addr, [int(value)])
 925            return
 926        if resolved.scheme == "basic-byte":
 927            addr = _require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr")
 928            self.write_bytes(addr, [int(value)])
 929            return
 930        if resolved.scheme == "program-bit":
 931            no = _require(resolved.no, "program number")
 932            bit_no = _require(resolved.bit_no, "program bit")
 933            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "program addr")
 934            self.write_ext_multi([(no, bit_no, addr, int(value) & 0x01)], [], [])
 935            return
 936        if resolved.scheme == "program-word":
 937            no = _require(resolved.no, "program number")
 938            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "program addr")
 939            self.write_ext_words(no, addr, [int(value)])
 940            return
 941        if resolved.scheme == "program-byte":
 942            no = _require(resolved.no, "program number")
 943            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "program addr")
 944            self.write_ext_bytes(no, addr, [int(value)])
 945            return
 946        if resolved.scheme == "ext-bit":
 947            no = _require(resolved.no, "extended number")
 948            bit_no = _require(resolved.bit_no, "extended bit")
 949            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr")
 950            self.write_ext_multi([(no, bit_no, addr, int(value) & 0x01)], [], [])
 951            return
 952        if resolved.scheme == "ext-word":
 953            no = _require(resolved.no, "extended number")
 954            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr")
 955            self.write_ext_words(no, addr, [int(value)])
 956            return
 957        if resolved.scheme == "ext-byte":
 958            no = _require(resolved.no, "extended number")
 959            addr = _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr")
 960            self.write_ext_bytes(no, addr, [int(value)])
 961            return
 962        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-bit":
 963            addr32 = _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32")
 964            self.pc10_multi_write(_pack_pc10_multi_bit_payload([(addr32, int(value) & 0x01)]))
 965            return
 966        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-word":
 967            addr32 = _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32")
 968            _write_pc10_block_word(self, addr32, int(value))
 969            return
 970        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-byte":
 971            addr32 = _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32")
 972            self.pc10_block_write(addr32, bytes([int(value) & 0xFF]))
 973            return
 974        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported resolved scheme: {resolved.scheme}")
 975
 976    def _relay_write_one(self, hops, resolved: ResolvedDevice, value) -> None:
 977        if resolved.scheme == "basic-bit":
 978            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_bit_write(_require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr"), int(value) & 0x01))
 979            if resp.cmd != 0x21:
 980                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay bit-write response")
 981            return
 982        if resolved.scheme == "basic-word":
 983            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_word_write(_require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr"), [int(value)]))
 984            if resp.cmd != 0x1D:
 985                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay word-write response")
 986            return
 987        if resolved.scheme == "basic-byte":
 988            resp = self.send_via_relay(hops, build_byte_write(_require(resolved.basic_addr, "basic_addr"), [int(value)]))
 989            if resp.cmd != 0x1F:
 990                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay byte-write response")
 991            return
 992        if resolved.scheme == "program-bit":
 993            resp = self.send_via_relay(
 994                hops,
 995                build_ext_multi_write(
 996                    [(_require(resolved.no, "program number"), _require(resolved.bit_no, "program bit"), _require(resolved.addr, "program addr"), int(value) & 0x01)],
 997                    [],
 998                    [],
 999                ),
1000            )
1001            if resp.cmd != 0x99:
1002                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay multi-write response")
1003            return
1004        if resolved.scheme == "program-word":
1005            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1006                hops,
1007                build_ext_word_write(_require(resolved.no, "program number"), _require(resolved.addr, "program addr"), [int(value)]),
1008            )
1009            if resp.cmd != 0x95:
1010                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext word-write response")
1011            return
1012        if resolved.scheme == "program-byte":
1013            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1014                hops,
1015                build_ext_byte_write(_require(resolved.no, "program number"), _require(resolved.addr, "program addr"), [int(value)]),
1016            )
1017            if resp.cmd != 0x97:
1018                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext byte-write response")
1019            return
1020        if resolved.scheme == "ext-bit":
1021            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1022                hops,
1023                build_ext_multi_write(
1024                    [(_require(resolved.no, "extended number"), _require(resolved.bit_no, "extended bit"), _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr"), int(value) & 0x01)],
1025                    [],
1026                    [],
1027                ),
1028            )
1029            if resp.cmd != 0x99:
1030                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay multi-write response")
1031            return
1032        if resolved.scheme == "ext-word":
1033            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1034                hops,
1035                build_ext_word_write(_require(resolved.no, "extended number"), _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr"), [int(value)]),
1036            )
1037            if resp.cmd != 0x95:
1038                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext word-write response")
1039            return
1040        if resolved.scheme == "ext-byte":
1041            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1042                hops,
1043                build_ext_byte_write(_require(resolved.no, "extended number"), _require(resolved.addr, "extended addr"), [int(value)]),
1044            )
1045            if resp.cmd != 0x97:
1046                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay ext byte-write response")
1047            return
1048        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-bit":
1049            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1050                hops,
1051                build_pc10_multi_write(_pack_pc10_multi_bit_payload([(_require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32"), int(value) & 0x01)])),
1052            )
1053            if resp.cmd != 0xC5:
1054                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay PC10 multi-write response")
1055            return
1056        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-word":
1057            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1058                hops,
1059                build_pc10_block_write(
1060                    _require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32"),
1061                    (int(value) & 0xFFFF).to_bytes(2, "little"),
1062                ),
1063            )
1064            if resp.cmd != 0xC3:
1065                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay PC10 block-write response")
1066            return
1067        if resolved.scheme == "pc10-byte":
1068            resp = self.send_via_relay(
1069                hops,
1070                build_pc10_block_write(_require(resolved.addr32, "pc10 addr32"), bytes([int(value) & 0xFF])),
1071            )
1072            if resp.cmd != 0xC3:
1073                raise ToyopucProtocolError("Unexpected CMD in relay PC10 block-write response")
1074            return
1075        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported resolved scheme: {resolved.scheme}")
1076
1077    def _offset(self, resolved: ResolvedDevice, delta: int) -> ResolvedDevice:
1078        if delta == 0:
1079            return resolved
1080        width = resolved.digits if resolved.digits > 0 else max(4, len(f"{resolved.index:X}"))
1081        if resolved.unit == "byte":
1082            suffix = "H" if resolved.high else "L"
1083            index = resolved.index + delta
1084            if resolved.prefix:
1085                return resolve_device(f"{resolved.prefix}-{resolved.area}{index:0{width}X}{suffix}")
1086            return resolve_device(f"{resolved.area}{index:0{width}X}{suffix}")
1087        index = resolved.index + delta
1088        suffix = "W" if resolved.packed and resolved.unit == "word" else ""
1089        if resolved.prefix:
1090            return resolve_device(f"{resolved.prefix}-{resolved.area}{index:0{width}X}{suffix}")
1091        return resolve_device(f"{resolved.area}{index:0{width}X}{suffix}")

High-level client that accepts string device addresses.

This wrapper hides branching between basic, prefixed, extended, and PC10 access paths. It is the default client for application code.

Use this class when you want the library to choose the correct low-level command family from a string such as D0000, P1-M0000, EX0010W, or GX0010H.

def read_cpu_status(self): 
452    def read_cpu_status(self):
453        return super().read_cpu_status()

Read the 8-byte CPU status block via CMD=32 / 11 00.

def relay_read_cpu_status(self, hops): 
455    def relay_read_cpu_status(self, hops):
456        return super().relay_read_cpu_status(hops)

Read the 8-byte CPU status block through relay hops.

def read_cpu_status_a0(self): 
458    def read_cpu_status_a0(self):
459        return super().read_cpu_status_a0()

Read decoded CPU status via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

def relay_read_cpu_status_a0(self, hops): 
461    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0(self, hops):
462        return super().relay_read_cpu_status_a0(hops)

Read decoded CPU status through relay hops via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

def read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self): 
464    def read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self):
465        return super().read_cpu_status_a0_raw()

Read raw 8-byte CPU status via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

This command path is used in the flash/FR completion flow. The library currently returns the 8 raw status bytes because the exact bit mapping for this path has not been finalized yet.

def relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self, hops): 
467    def relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(self, hops):
468        return super().relay_read_cpu_status_a0_raw(hops)

Read raw 8-byte CPU status through relay hops via CMD=A0 / 01 10.

def read_clock(self): 
470    def read_clock(self):
471        return super().read_clock()

Read the CPU clock via CMD=32 / 70 00.

def relay_read_clock(self, hops): 
473    def relay_read_clock(self, hops):
474        return super().relay_read_clock(hops)

Read the CPU clock through relay hops.

def relay_write_clock(self, hops, value: datetime.datetime) -> None: 
476    def relay_write_clock(self, hops, value: datetime) -> None:
477        super().relay_write_clock(hops, value)

Set the CPU clock through relay hops via CMD=32 / 71 00.

def write_clock(self, value: datetime.datetime) -> None: 
479    def write_clock(self, value: datetime) -> None:
480        super().write_clock(value)

Set the CPU clock via CMD=32 / 71 00.

def resolve_device(self, device: str) -> ResolvedDevice: 
482    def resolve_device(self, device: str) -> ResolvedDevice:
483        """Resolve a string address into a `ResolvedDevice`."""
484        return resolve_device(device)

Resolve a string address into a ResolvedDevice.

def relay_read( self, hops, device: str | ResolvedDevice, count: int = 1): 
486    def relay_read(self, hops, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
487        """Read one item or a contiguous sequence through relay hops."""
488        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
489        if count < 1:
490            raise ValueError("count must be >= 1")
491        if count == 1:
492            return self._relay_read_one(hops, resolved)
493        return [self._relay_read_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i)) for i in range(count)]

Read one item or a contiguous sequence through relay hops.

def relay_write( self, hops, device: str | ResolvedDevice, value) -> None: 
495    def relay_write(self, hops, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], value) -> None:
496        """Write one item or a contiguous sequence through relay hops."""
497        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
498        if resolved.unit == "bit":
499            if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
500                for i, item in enumerate(value):
501                    self._relay_write_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i), item)
502                return
503            self._relay_write_one(hops, resolved, value)
504            return
505
506        if isinstance(value, (bytes, bytearray)):
507            for i, item in enumerate(value):
508                self._relay_write_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i), item)
509            return
510        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
511            for i, item in enumerate(value):
512                self._relay_write_one(hops, self._offset(resolved, i), item)
513            return
514        self._relay_write_one(hops, resolved, value)

Write one item or a contiguous sequence through relay hops.

def relay_read_words( self, hops: str | Iterable[tuple[int, int]], device: int | str | ResolvedDevice, count: int = 1) -> List[int]: 
516    def relay_read_words(
517        self,
518        hops: str | Iterable[tuple[int, int]],
519        device: Union[int, str, ResolvedDevice],
520        count: int = 1,
521    ) -> List[int]:
522        """Read one or more word devices through relay hops."""
523        if isinstance(device, int):
524            return super().relay_read_words(hops, device, count)
525        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
526        if resolved.unit != "word":
527            raise ValueError("relay_read_words() requires a word device")
528        values = self.relay_read(hops, resolved, count)
529        if isinstance(values, list):
530            return [int(item) for item in values]
531        return [int(values)]

Read one or more word devices through relay hops.

def relay_write_words( self, hops: str | Iterable[tuple[int, int]], device: int | str | ResolvedDevice, value: Iterable[int] | int) -> None: 
533    def relay_write_words(
534        self,
535        hops: str | Iterable[tuple[int, int]],
536        device: Union[int, str, ResolvedDevice],
537        value: Union[Iterable[int], int],
538    ) -> None:
539        """Write one or more word devices through relay hops."""
540        if isinstance(device, int):
541            if isinstance(value, int):
542                super().relay_write_words(hops, device, [value])
543            else:
544                super().relay_write_words(hops, device, value)
545            return
546        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
547        if resolved.unit != "word":
548            raise ValueError("relay_write_words() requires a word device")
549        self.relay_write(hops, resolved, value)

Write one or more word devices through relay hops.

def relay_read_many( self, hops, devices: Sequence[str | ResolvedDevice]) -> List[object]: 
551    def relay_read_many(self, hops, devices: Sequence[Union[str, ResolvedDevice]]) -> List[object]:
552        """Read multiple devices through relay hops and preserve input order."""
553        return [self.relay_read(hops, device) for device in devices]

Read multiple devices through relay hops and preserve input order.

def relay_write_many( self, hops, items: Mapping[str | ResolvedDevice, object]) -> None: 
555    def relay_write_many(self, hops, items: Mapping[Union[str, ResolvedDevice], object]) -> None:
556        """Write multiple devices through relay hops in input order."""
557        for device, value in items.items():
558            self.relay_write(hops, device, value)

Write multiple devices through relay hops in input order.

def read_fr( self, device: str | ResolvedDevice, count: int = 1): 
560    def read_fr(self, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
561        """Read one or more FR words using the dedicated FR path."""
562        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
563        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
564            raise ValueError("read_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
565        values = self.read_fr_words(resolved.index, count)
566        return values[0] if count == 1 else values

Read one or more FR words using the dedicated FR path.

def relay_read_fr( self, hops, device: str | ResolvedDevice, count: int = 1): 
568    def relay_read_fr(self, hops, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
569        """Read one or more FR words through relay hops."""
570        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
571        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
572            raise ValueError("relay_read_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
573        return self.relay_read(hops, resolved, count)

Read one or more FR words through relay hops.

def write_fr( self, device: str | ResolvedDevice, value, *, commit: bool = False, wait: bool | None = None, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> None: 
575    def write_fr(
576        self,
577        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
578        value,
579        *,
580        commit: bool = False,
581        wait: Optional[bool] = None,
582        timeout: float = 30.0,
583        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
584    ) -> None:
585        """Write one or more FR words, optionally committing and waiting."""
586        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
587        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
588            raise ValueError("write_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
589        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
590            values = [int(item) for item in value]
591        else:
592            values = [int(value)]
593        should_wait = bool(commit) if wait is None else bool(wait)
594        self.write_fr_words_ex(
595            resolved.index,
596            values,
597            commit=commit,
598            wait=should_wait,
599            timeout=timeout,
600            poll_interval=poll_interval,
601        )

Write one or more FR words, optionally committing and waiting.

def relay_write_fr( self, hops, device: str | ResolvedDevice, value, *, commit: bool = False, wait: bool | None = None, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> None: 
603    def relay_write_fr(
604        self,
605        hops,
606        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
607        value,
608        *,
609        commit: bool = False,
610        wait: Optional[bool] = None,
611        timeout: float = 30.0,
612        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
613    ) -> None:
614        """Write one or more FR words through relay hops, optionally committing."""
615        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
616        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
617            raise ValueError("relay_write_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
618        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
619            values = [int(item) for item in value]
620        else:
621            values = [int(value)]
622        should_wait = bool(commit) if wait is None else bool(wait)
623        self.relay_write_fr_words_ex(
624            hops,
625            resolved.index,
626            values,
627            commit=commit,
628            wait=should_wait,
629            timeout=timeout,
630            poll_interval=poll_interval,
631        )

Write one or more FR words through relay hops, optionally committing.

def commit_fr( self, device: str | ResolvedDevice, count: int = 1, *, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> None: 
633    def commit_fr(
634        self,
635        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
636        count: int = 1,
637        *,
638        wait: bool = False,
639        timeout: float = 30.0,
640        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
641    ) -> None:
642        """Commit every FR block touched by the given FR word range."""
643        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
644        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
645            raise ValueError("commit_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
646        self.commit_fr_range(
647            resolved.index,
648            count,
649            wait=wait,
650            timeout=timeout,
651            poll_interval=poll_interval,
652        )

Commit every FR block touched by the given FR word range.

def relay_commit_fr( self, hops, device: str | ResolvedDevice, count: int = 1, *, wait: bool = False, timeout: float = 30.0, poll_interval: float = 0.2) -> None: 
654    def relay_commit_fr(
655        self,
656        hops,
657        device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice],
658        count: int = 1,
659        *,
660        wait: bool = False,
661        timeout: float = 30.0,
662        poll_interval: float = 0.2,
663    ) -> None:
664        """Commit every FR block touched by the given FR word range through relay hops."""
665        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
666        if resolved.area != "FR" or resolved.unit != "word":
667            raise ValueError("relay_commit_fr() requires an FR word device such as FR000000")
668        self.relay_commit_fr_range(
669            hops,
670            resolved.index,
671            count,
672            wait=wait,
673            timeout=timeout,
674            poll_interval=poll_interval,
675        )

Commit every FR block touched by the given FR word range through relay hops.

def read( self, device: str | ResolvedDevice, count: int = 1): 
677    def read(self, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], count: int = 1):
678        """Read one item or a contiguous sequence from a device address.
679
680        Args:
681            device: String address or previously resolved device descriptor.
682            count: Number of contiguous items to read.
683
684        Returns:
685            A scalar when ``count == 1``. Otherwise a list of values in device
686            order.
687        """
688        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
689        if count < 1:
690            raise ValueError("count must be >= 1")
691        if count == 1:
692            return self._read_one(resolved)
693        return [self._read_one(self._offset(resolved, i)) for i in range(count)]

Read one item or a contiguous sequence from a device address.

Args: device: String address or previously resolved device descriptor. count: Number of contiguous items to read.

Returns: A scalar when count == 1. Otherwise a list of values in device order.

def write(self, device: str | ResolvedDevice, value) -> None: 
695    def write(self, device: Union[str, ResolvedDevice], value) -> None:
696        """Write one item or a contiguous sequence to a device address.
697
698        Args:
699            device: String address or previously resolved device descriptor.
700            value: Scalar, sequence, or bytes-like object depending on the
701                target unit.
702        """
703        resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
704        if resolved.area == "FR":
705            _raise_generic_fr_write_error()
706        if resolved.unit == "bit":
707            if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
708                for i, item in enumerate(value):
709                    self._write_one(self._offset(resolved, i), item)
710                return
711            self._write_one(resolved, value)
712            return
713
714        if isinstance(value, (bytes, bytearray)):
715            for i, item in enumerate(value):
716                self._write_one(self._offset(resolved, i), item)
717            return
718        if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
719            for i, item in enumerate(value):
720                self._write_one(self._offset(resolved, i), item)
721            return
722        self._write_one(resolved, value)

Write one item or a contiguous sequence to a device address.

Args: device: String address or previously resolved device descriptor. value: Scalar, sequence, or bytes-like object depending on the target unit.

def read_many( self, devices: Sequence[str | ResolvedDevice]) -> List[object]: 
724    def read_many(self, devices: Sequence[Union[str, ResolvedDevice]]) -> List[object]:
725        """Read multiple devices and preserve input order.
726
727        Args:
728            devices: Sequence of string addresses or resolved device objects.
729
730        Returns:
731            List of values in the same order as ``devices``.
732
733        Notes:
734            This method currently favors simple, predictable dispatch over
735            grouped transport optimization.
736        """
737        resolved = [self.resolve_device(d) if isinstance(d, str) else d for d in devices]
738        return [self._read_one(item) for item in resolved]

Read multiple devices and preserve input order.

Args: devices: Sequence of string addresses or resolved device objects.

Returns: List of values in the same order as devices.

Notes: This method currently favors simple, predictable dispatch over grouped transport optimization.

def write_many( self, items: Mapping[str | ResolvedDevice, object]) -> None: 
740    def write_many(self, items: Mapping[Union[str, ResolvedDevice], object]) -> None:
741        """Write multiple devices in mapping iteration order.
742
743        Args:
744            items: Mapping of device -> value pairs.
745
746        Notes:
747            This method currently dispatches each item independently.
748        """
749        resolved_items = []
750        for device, value in items.items():
751            resolved = self.resolve_device(device) if isinstance(device, str) else device
752            if resolved.area == "FR":
753                _raise_generic_fr_write_error()
754            resolved_items.append((resolved, value))
755        for resolved, value in resolved_items:
756            self._write_one(resolved, value)

Write multiple devices in mapping iteration order.

Args: items: Mapping of device -> value pairs.

Notes: This method currently dispatches each item independently.

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResolvedDevice: 
 75@dataclass(frozen=True)
 76class ResolvedDevice:
 77    """Resolved high-level device description.
 78
 79    This is the normalized result of `resolve_device()`. It records which
 80    command family should be used and the encoded address fields needed for it.
 81
 82    Attributes:
 83        text: Original normalized device string.
 84        scheme: Selected access path such as ``basic-word``, ``program-bit``,
 85            ``ext-byte``, or ``pc10-word``.
 86        unit: Resolved access unit: ``bit``, ``byte``, or ``word``.
 87        area: Device family such as ``D``, ``M``, ``EX``, or ``GX``.
 88        index: Numeric address value interpreted as hexadecimal.
 89        prefix: Optional ``P1``/``P2``/``P3`` program prefix.
 90        high: ``True`` when the resolved byte address uses the upper-byte
 91            designator ``H``.
 92        packed: ``True`` when the address used ``W/H/L`` notation for a
 93            bit-device family.
 94        basic_addr: Encoded basic-area address used by ``CMD=1C``-``25``.
 95        no: Extended/program number used by ``CMD=94``-``99``.
 96        addr: 16-bit address field used with ``no``.
 97        bit_no: Bit position used by extended/program bit access.
 98        addr32: 32-bit PC10 address used by ``CMD=C2``-``C5``.
 99    """
100
101    text: str
102    scheme: str
103    unit: str
104    area: str
105    index: int
106    digits: int = 0
107    prefix: Optional[str] = None
108    high: bool = False
109    packed: bool = False
110    basic_addr: Optional[int] = None
111    no: Optional[int] = None
112    addr: Optional[int] = None
113    bit_no: Optional[int] = None
114    addr32: Optional[int] = None

Resolved high-level device description.

This is the normalized result of resolve_device(). It records which command family should be used and the encoded address fields needed for it.

Attributes: text: Original normalized device string. scheme: Selected access path such as basic-word, program-bit, ext-byte, or pc10-word. unit: Resolved access unit: bit, byte, or word. area: Device family such as D, M, EX, or GX. index: Numeric address value interpreted as hexadecimal. prefix: Optional P1/P2/P3 program prefix. high: True when the resolved byte address uses the upper-byte designator H. packed: True when the address used W/H/L notation for a bit-device family. basic_addr: Encoded basic-area address used by CMD=1C-25. no: Extended/program number used by CMD=94-99. addr: 16-bit address field used with no. bit_no: Bit position used by extended/program bit access. addr32: 32-bit PC10 address used by CMD=C2-C5.

ResolvedDevice( text: str, scheme: str, unit: str, area: str, index: int, digits: int = 0, prefix: str | None = None, high: bool = False, packed: bool = False, basic_addr: int | None = None, no: int | None = None, addr: int | None = None, bit_no: int | None = None, addr32: int | None = None)
text: str
scheme: str
unit: str
area: str
index: int
digits: int = 0
prefix: str | None = None
high: bool = False
packed: bool = False
basic_addr: int | None = None
no: int | None = None
addr: int | None = None
bit_no: int | None = None
addr32: int | None = None
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ClockData: 
26@dataclass(frozen=True)
27class ClockData:
28    """Raw PLC clock fields returned by ``CMD=32 / 70 00``.
29
30    Attributes:
31        second: BCD-decoded seconds field.
32        minute: BCD-decoded minutes field.
33        hour: BCD-decoded hour field in 24-hour format.
34        day: BCD-decoded day-of-month field.
35        month: BCD-decoded month field. Some models can report ``0`` when the
36            calendar part is unset.
37        year_2digit: Lower two digits of the year.
38        weekday: PLC weekday value where ``0`` means Sunday.
39    """
40
41    second: int
42    minute: int
43    hour: int
44    day: int
45    month: int
46    year_2digit: int
47    weekday: int
48
49    def as_datetime(self, *, year_base: int = 2000) -> datetime:
50        year = year_base + self.year_2digit
51        return datetime(year, self.month, self.day, self.hour, self.minute, self.second)

Raw PLC clock fields returned by CMD=32 / 70 00.

Attributes: second: BCD-decoded seconds field. minute: BCD-decoded minutes field. hour: BCD-decoded hour field in 24-hour format. day: BCD-decoded day-of-month field. month: BCD-decoded month field. Some models can report 0 when the calendar part is unset. year_2digit: Lower two digits of the year. weekday: PLC weekday value where 0 means Sunday.

ClockData( second: int, minute: int, hour: int, day: int, month: int, year_2digit: int, weekday: int)
second: int
minute: int
hour: int
day: int
month: int
year_2digit: int
weekday: int
def as_datetime(self, *, year_base: int = 2000) -> datetime.datetime: 
49    def as_datetime(self, *, year_base: int = 2000) -> datetime:
50        year = year_base + self.year_2digit
51        return datetime(year, self.month, self.day, self.hour, self.minute, self.second)
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CpuStatusData: 
 54@dataclass(frozen=True)
 55class CpuStatusData:
 56    """Decoded container for the 8 CPU-status bytes from ``CMD=32 / 11 00``.
 57
 58    The ``data1``-``data8`` fields store the raw status bytes. The boolean
 59    properties expose the manual-defined flag meanings such as ``run``,
 60    ``alarm``, and ``program1_running``.
 61    """
 62
 63    data1: int
 64    data2: int
 65    data3: int
 66    data4: int
 67    data5: int
 68    data6: int
 69    data7: int
 70    data8: int
 71
 72    @property
 73    def raw_bytes(self) -> bytes:
 74        return bytes(
 75            [
 76                self.data1,
 77                self.data2,
 78                self.data3,
 79                self.data4,
 80                self.data5,
 81                self.data6,
 82                self.data7,
 83                self.data8,
 84            ]
 85        )
 86
 87    @property
 88    def raw_bytes_hex(self) -> str:
 89        return " ".join(f"{b:02X}" for b in self.raw_bytes)
 90
 91    def raw_hex(self) -> str:
 92        return self.raw_bytes_hex
 93
 94    @property
 95    def run(self) -> bool:
 96        return bool(self.data1 & 0x80)
 97
 98    @property
 99    def under_stop(self) -> bool:
100        return bool(self.data1 & 0x40)
101
102    @property
103    def under_stop_request_continuity(self) -> bool:
104        return bool(self.data1 & 0x20)
105
106    @property
107    def under_pseudo_stop(self) -> bool:
108        return bool(self.data1 & 0x10)
109
110    @property
111    def debug_mode(self) -> bool:
112        return bool(self.data1 & 0x08)
113
114    @property
115    def io_monitor_user_mode(self) -> bool:
116        return bool(self.data1 & 0x04)
117
118    @property
119    def pc3_mode(self) -> bool:
120        return bool(self.data1 & 0x02)
121
122    @property
123    def pc10_mode(self) -> bool:
124        return bool(self.data1 & 0x01)
125
126    @property
127    def fatal_failure(self) -> bool:
128        return bool(self.data2 & 0x80)
129
130    @property
131    def faint_failure(self) -> bool:
132        return bool(self.data2 & 0x40)
133
134    @property
135    def alarm(self) -> bool:
136        return bool(self.data2 & 0x20)
137
138    @property
139    def io_allocation_parameter_altered(self) -> bool:
140        return bool(self.data2 & 0x08)
141
142    @property
143    def with_memory_card(self) -> bool:
144        return bool(self.data2 & 0x04)
145
146    @property
147    def memory_card_operation(self) -> bool:
148        return bool(self.data3 & 0x80)
149
150    @property
151    def write_protected_program_info(self) -> bool:
152        return bool(self.data3 & 0x40)
153
154    @property
155    def read_protected_system_memory(self) -> bool:
156        return bool(self.data4 & 0x80)
157
158    @property
159    def write_protected_system_memory(self) -> bool:
160        return bool(self.data4 & 0x40)
161
162    @property
163    def read_protected_system_io(self) -> bool:
164        return bool(self.data4 & 0x20)
165
166    @property
167    def write_protected_system_io(self) -> bool:
168        return bool(self.data4 & 0x10)
169
170    @property
171    def trace(self) -> bool:
172        return bool(self.data5 & 0x80)
173
174    @property
175    def scan_sampling_trace(self) -> bool:
176        return bool(self.data5 & 0x40)
177
178    @property
179    def periodic_sampling_trace(self) -> bool:
180        return bool(self.data5 & 0x20)
181
182    @property
183    def enable_detected(self) -> bool:
184        return bool(self.data5 & 0x10)
185
186    @property
187    def trigger_detected(self) -> bool:
188        return bool(self.data5 & 0x08)
189
190    @property
191    def one_scan_step(self) -> bool:
192        return bool(self.data5 & 0x04)
193
194    @property
195    def one_block_step(self) -> bool:
196        return bool(self.data5 & 0x02)
197
198    @property
199    def one_instruction_step(self) -> bool:
200        return bool(self.data5 & 0x01)
201
202    @property
203    def io_offline(self) -> bool:
204        return bool(self.data6 & 0x80)
205
206    @property
207    def remote_run_setting(self) -> bool:
208        return bool(self.data6 & 0x40)
209
210    @property
211    def status_latch_setting(self) -> bool:
212        return bool(self.data6 & 0x20)
213
214    @property
215    def write_priority_limited_program_info(self) -> bool:
216        return bool(self.data7 & 0x40)
217
218    @property
219    def abnormal_write_flash_register(self) -> bool:
220        return bool(self.data7 & 0x20)
221
222    @property
223    def under_writing_flash_register(self) -> bool:
224        return bool(self.data7 & 0x10)
225
226    @property
227    def abnormal_write_equipment_info(self) -> bool:
228        return bool(self.data7 & 0x08)
229
230    @property
231    def abnormal_writing_equipment_info(self) -> bool:
232        return bool(self.data7 & 0x04)
233
234    @property
235    def abnormal_write_during_run(self) -> bool:
236        return bool(self.data7 & 0x02)
237
238    @property
239    def under_writing_during_run(self) -> bool:
240        return bool(self.data7 & 0x01)
241
242    @property
243    def program3_running(self) -> bool:
244        return bool(self.data8 & 0x08)
245
246    @property
247    def program2_running(self) -> bool:
248        return bool(self.data8 & 0x04)
249
250    @property
251    def program1_running(self) -> bool:
252        return bool(self.data8 & 0x02)

Decoded container for the 8 CPU-status bytes from CMD=32 / 11 00.

The data1-data8 fields store the raw status bytes. The boolean properties expose the manual-defined flag meanings such as run, alarm, and program1_running.

CpuStatusData( data1: int, data2: int, data3: int, data4: int, data5: int, data6: int, data7: int, data8: int)
data1: int
data2: int
data3: int
data4: int
data5: int
data6: int
data7: int
data8: int
raw_bytes: bytes 
72    @property
73    def raw_bytes(self) -> bytes:
74        return bytes(
75            [
76                self.data1,
77                self.data2,
78                self.data3,
79                self.data4,
80                self.data5,
81                self.data6,
82                self.data7,
83                self.data8,
84            ]
85        )
raw_bytes_hex: str 
87    @property
88    def raw_bytes_hex(self) -> str:
89        return " ".join(f"{b:02X}" for b in self.raw_bytes)
def raw_hex(self) -> str: 
91    def raw_hex(self) -> str:
92        return self.raw_bytes_hex
run: bool 
94    @property
95    def run(self) -> bool:
96        return bool(self.data1 & 0x80)

 98    @property

100        return bool(self.data1 & 0x40)
