@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RelayLayer: 
12@dataclass(frozen=True)
13class RelayLayer:
14    """One decoded relay wrapper layer from a `CMD=60` response."""
15
16    link_no: int
17    station_no: int
18    ack: int
19    inner_raw: bytes
20    padding: bytes = b""

One decoded relay wrapper layer from a CMD=60 response.

RelayLayer( link_no: int, station_no: int, ack: int, inner_raw: bytes, padding: bytes = b'')
link_no: int
station_no: int
ack: int
inner_raw: bytes
padding: bytes = b''
class ToyopucError(builtins.Exception): 
3class ToyopucError(Exception):
4    """Base error for TOYOPUC communication."""

Base error for TOYOPUC communication.

class ToyopucProtocolError(toyopuc.ToyopucError): 
7class ToyopucProtocolError(ToyopucError):
8    """Raised when the response frame is invalid or unexpected."""

Raised when the response frame is invalid or unexpected.

class ToyopucTimeoutError(toyopuc.ToyopucError): 
11class ToyopucTimeoutError(ToyopucError):
12    """Raised on socket timeouts."""

Raised on socket timeouts.

def parse_address( text: str, unit: str, *, radix: int = 16) -> toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress: 
268def parse_address(text: str, unit: str, *, radix: int = 16) -> ParsedAddress:
269    """Parse address strings like 'D0100', 'D0100L', 'M0201'.
270
271    Notes:
272    - The manual examples use hexadecimal numeric fields (e.g. D0100 -> 0x0100).
273    - Use radix=10 if your PLC uses decimal notation.
274    """
275    m = _ADDR_RE.match(text.strip().upper())
276    if not m:
277        raise ValueError(f'Invalid address format: {text!r}')
278
279    area = m.group('area')
280    num_text = m.group('num')
281    num = int(num_text, radix)
282    suffix = m.group('suffix')
283
284    if unit == 'byte' and suffix is None:
285        # Default to low byte when omitted for byte access.
286        suffix = 'L'
287    if unit == 'byte':
288        if suffix not in ('L', 'H'):
289            raise ValueError(f'L/H suffix required for byte unit: {text!r}')
290    elif unit == 'word':
291        if suffix not in (None, 'W'):
292            raise ValueError(f'W suffix only valid for packed word notation: {text!r}')
293    else:
294        if suffix is not None:
295            raise ValueError(f'Suffix only valid for byte/packed-word notation: {text!r}')
296
297    if unit == 'bit':
298        _validate_bit_index(area, num)
299    elif suffix == 'W' or (unit == 'byte' and (area in _BASIC_BIT_SEGMENTS or area in _EXT_BIT_SEGMENTS)):
300        _validate_packed_digits(area, text, len(num_text))
301        _validate_packed_index(area, num, prefixed=False, text=text)
302
303    return ParsedAddress(
304        area=area,
305        index=num,
306        unit=unit,
307        high=(suffix == 'H'),
308        packed=(suffix == 'W'),
309        digits=len(num_text),
310    )

Parse address strings like 'D0100', 'D0100L', 'M0201'.

Notes:

  • The manual examples use hexadecimal numeric fields (e.g. D0100 -> 0x0100).
  • Use radix=10 if your PLC uses decimal notation.
def parse_prefixed_address( text: str, unit: str, *, radix: int = 16) -> Tuple[int, toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress]: 
313def parse_prefixed_address(text: str, unit: str, *, radix: int = 16) -> Tuple[int, ParsedAddress]:
314    """Parse a prefixed address and return `(program_ex_no, parsed_address)`.
315
316    Examples:
317    - `P1-M1000`
318    - `P2-D2000L`
319    - `P3-X0010H`
320
321    The returned `ex_no` is the prefix-side program exchange number used by
322    prefixed access paths. The second value is a normal `ParsedAddress`.
323    """
324    m = _PREF_RE.match(text.strip().upper())
325    if not m:
326        raise ValueError(f'Invalid prefixed address format: {text!r}')
327
328    prefix = m.group('prefix')
329    area = m.group('area')
330    num_text = m.group('num')
331    num = int(num_text, radix)
332    suffix = m.group('suffix')
333
334    if unit == 'byte' and suffix is None:
335        suffix = 'L'
336    if unit == 'byte':
337        if suffix not in ('L', 'H'):
338            raise ValueError(f'L/H suffix required for byte unit: {text!r}')
339    elif unit == 'word':
340        if suffix not in (None, 'W'):
341            raise ValueError(f'W suffix only valid for packed word notation: {text!r}')
342    else:
343        if suffix is not None:
344            raise ValueError(f'Suffix only valid for byte/packed-word notation: {text!r}')
345
346    if unit == 'bit':
347        if area not in _PROGRAM_BIT_SEGMENTS:
348            raise ValueError(f'Unsupported program bit area: {area}')
349        if not _in_segments(num, [(start, end) for start, end, _ in _PROGRAM_BIT_SEGMENTS[area]]):
350            raise ValueError(f'Program bit address out of range: {area}{num:04X}')
351    elif suffix == 'W' or (unit == 'byte' and area in _PROGRAM_BIT_SEGMENTS):
352        _validate_packed_digits(area, text, len(num_text))
353        _validate_packed_index(area, num, prefixed=True, text=text)
354
355    ex_no = _P_EXNO[prefix]
356    return ex_no, ParsedAddress(
357        area=area,
358        index=num,
359        unit=unit,
360        high=(suffix == 'H'),
361        packed=(suffix == 'W'),
362        digits=len(num_text),
363    )

Parse a prefixed address and return (program_ex_no, parsed_address).

Examples:

  • P1-M1000
  • P2-D2000L
  • P3-X0010H

The returned ex_no is the prefix-side program exchange number used by prefixed access paths. The second value is a normal ParsedAddress.

def encode_word_address(addr: toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress) -> int: 
366def encode_word_address(addr: ParsedAddress) -> int:
367    """Encode a basic-area word address into the numeric protocol address.
368
369    This is used for normal word commands such as `CMD=1C/1D`.
370    For bit-device families, `...W` notation is accepted and mapped to the
371    corresponding word address.
372    """
373    if addr.unit != 'word':
374        raise ValueError('Expected word address')
375    if addr.packed and addr.area not in _BIT_BASE:
376        raise ValueError(f'W suffix is only valid for bit-device families: {addr.area}{addr.index:X}W')
377    if addr.packed and not _in_segments(addr.index, _BASIC_PACKED_SEGMENTS[addr.area]):
378        raise ValueError(f'Packed W address out of range: {addr.area}{addr.index:03X}W')
379    base = _WORD_BASE.get(addr.area)
380    if base is None:
381        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported word area: {addr.area}')
382    return base + addr.index

Encode a basic-area word address into the numeric protocol address.

This is used for normal word commands such as CMD=1C/1D. For bit-device families, ...W notation is accepted and mapped to the corresponding word address.

def encode_byte_address(addr: toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress) -> int: 
385def encode_byte_address(addr: ParsedAddress) -> int:
386    """Encode a basic-area byte address into the numeric protocol address.
387
388    This is used for normal byte commands such as `CMD=1E/1F`.
389    For bit-device families, `...L` and `...H` are treated as W/H/L addressing.
390    """
391    if addr.unit != 'byte':
392        raise ValueError('Expected byte address')
393    if addr.area in _BASIC_PACKED_SEGMENTS and not _in_segments(addr.index, _BASIC_PACKED_SEGMENTS[addr.area]):
394        suffix = 'H' if addr.high else 'L'
395        raise ValueError(f'Packed byte address out of range: {addr.area}{addr.index:03X}{suffix}')
396    base = _BYTE_BASE.get(addr.area)
397    if base is None:
398        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported byte area: {addr.area}')
399    return base + addr.index * 2 + (1 if addr.high else 0)

Encode a basic-area byte address into the numeric protocol address.

This is used for normal byte commands such as CMD=1E/1F. For bit-device families, ...L and ...H are treated as W/H/L addressing.

def encode_bit_address(addr: toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress) -> int: 
402def encode_bit_address(addr: ParsedAddress) -> int:
403    """Encode a basic-area bit address into the numeric protocol address.
404
405    This is used for normal bit commands such as `CMD=20/21`.
406    """
407    if addr.unit != 'bit':
408        raise ValueError('Expected bit address')
409    base = _BIT_BASE.get(addr.area)
410    if base is None:
411        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported bit area: {addr.area}')
412    if not _in_segments(addr.index, _BASIC_BIT_SEGMENTS[addr.area]):
413        raise ValueError(f'Bit address out of range for {addr.area}: {addr.area}{addr.index:04X}')
414    return base + addr.index

Encode a basic-area bit address into the numeric protocol address.

This is used for normal bit commands such as CMD=20/21.

def encode_program_word_address(addr: toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress) -> int: 
417def encode_program_word_address(addr: ParsedAddress) -> int:
418    """Encode a prefixed (`P1/P2/P3`) word address for `CMD=94/95`.
419
420    This also supports `...W` addressing on prefixed bit-device families.
421    """
422    if addr.unit != 'word':
423        raise ValueError('Expected word address')
424    if addr.packed and addr.area not in _PROGRAM_BIT_SEGMENTS:
425        raise ValueError(f'W suffix is only valid for prefixed bit-device families: {addr.area}{addr.index:X}W')
426    segments = _PROGRAM_WORD_SEGMENTS.get(addr.area)
427    if segments is None:
428        segments = _PROGRAM_BIT_WORD_SEGMENTS.get(addr.area)
429    if segments is None:
430        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported program word area: {addr.area}')
431    for start, end, base in segments:
432        if start <= addr.index <= end:
433            return base + (addr.index - start)
434    raise ValueError(f'Program word address out of range: {addr.area}{addr.index:04X}')

Encode a prefixed (P1/P2/P3) word address for CMD=94/95.

This also supports ...W addressing on prefixed bit-device families.

def encode_program_byte_address(addr: toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress) -> int: 
437def encode_program_byte_address(addr: ParsedAddress) -> int:
438    """Encode a prefixed (`P1/P2/P3`) byte address for `CMD=96/97`.
439
440    This also supports `...L` / `...H` addressing on prefixed bit-device
441    families.
442    """
443    if addr.unit != 'byte':
444        raise ValueError('Expected byte address')
445    segments = _PROGRAM_BYTE_SEGMENTS.get(addr.area)
446    if segments is None:
447        segments = _PROGRAM_BIT_BYTE_SEGMENTS.get(addr.area)
448    if segments is None:
449        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported program byte area: {addr.area}')
450    for start, end, base in segments:
451        if start <= addr.index <= end:
452            return base + (addr.index - start) * 2 + (1 if addr.high else 0)
453    suffix = 'H' if addr.high else 'L'
454    raise ValueError(f'Program byte address out of range: {addr.area}{addr.index:04X}{suffix}')

Encode a prefixed (P1/P2/P3) byte address for CMD=96/97.

This also supports ...L / ...H addressing on prefixed bit-device families.

def encode_program_bit_address(addr: toyopuc.address.ParsedAddress) -> Tuple[int, int]: 
457def encode_program_bit_address(addr: ParsedAddress) -> Tuple[int, int]:
458    """Encode a prefixed (`P1/P2/P3`) bit address for `CMD=98/99`.
459
460    Returns `(bit_no, addr)` where `bit_no` is the bit position inside the
461    addressed byte/word group and `addr` is the 16-bit monitor address field.
462    """
463    if addr.unit != 'bit':
464        raise ValueError('Expected bit address')
465    segments = _PROGRAM_BIT_SEGMENTS.get(addr.area)
466    if segments is None:
467        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported program bit area: {addr.area}')
468    for start, end, byte_base in segments:
469        if start <= addr.index <= end:
470            rel = addr.index - start
471            return rel & 0x07, byte_base + (rel >> 3)
472    raise ValueError(f'Program bit address out of range: {addr.area}{addr.index:04X}')

Encode a prefixed (P1/P2/P3) bit address for CMD=98/99.

Returns (bit_no, addr) where bit_no is the bit position inside the addressed byte/word group and addr is the 16-bit monitor address field.

def encode_exno_bit_u32(ex_no: int, bit_addr: int) -> int: 
475def encode_exno_bit_u32(ex_no: int, bit_addr: int) -> int:
476    """Encode a PC10 32-bit bit address from exchange number and bit address."""
477    return ((ex_no & 0xFF) << 19) | (bit_addr & 0x7FFFF)

Encode a PC10 32-bit bit address from exchange number and bit address.

def encode_exno_byte_u32(ex_no: int, byte_addr: int) -> int: 
480def encode_exno_byte_u32(ex_no: int, byte_addr: int) -> int:
481    """Encode a PC10 32-bit byte address from exchange number and byte address."""
482    return ((ex_no & 0xFF) << 16) | (byte_addr & 0xFFFF)

Encode a PC10 32-bit byte address from exchange number and byte address.

def split_u32_words(value: int) -> Tuple[int, int]: 
485def split_u32_words(value: int) -> Tuple[int, int]:
486    """Split a 32-bit value into `(low_word, high_word)`."""
487    low = value & 0xFFFF
488    high = (value >> 16) & 0xFFFF
489    return low, high

Split a 32-bit value into (low_word, high_word).

def encode_ext_no_address(area: str, index: int, unit: str) -> toyopuc.address.ExtNoAddress: 
515def encode_ext_no_address(area: str, index: int, unit: str) -> ExtNoAddress:
516    """Encode an extended-area address into `(No., 16-bit address)`.
517
518    This is the main helper for `CMD=94-99` on areas such as:
519    - `ES`, `EN`, `H`
520    - `U`, `EB`
521    - extended bit-device families when they are addressed by word/byte form
522
523    Note:
524    - real-hardware `FR` word access uses `encode_fr_word_addr32()` with
525      `CMD=C2/C3`, not `CMD=94-99`
526    """
527    area_u = area.upper()
528    no: Optional[int] = None
529    addr = index
530
531    if area_u in _EXT_AREA_MAP:
532        area_map = _EXT_AREA_MAP[area_u]
533        no = area_map['no']
534        if unit == 'word':
535            addr = area_map['word_base'] + index
536        elif unit == 'byte':
537            addr = area_map['byte_base'] + index
538        else:
539            raise ValueError(f'Unsupported unit for extended No mapping: {unit}')
540    elif area_u == 'EB':
541        # EB blocks are 0x8000 each
542        if index < 0x00000 or index > 0x3FFFF:
543            raise ValueError('EB index out of range (0x00000-0x3FFFF)')
544        block = index // 0x8000
545        no = 0x09 + block
546        addr = index % 0x8000
547    elif area_u == 'FR':
548        # FR blocks are 0x8000 each, Ex No 0x40-0x7F
549        if index < 0x000000 or index > 0x1FFFFF:
550            raise ValueError('FR index out of range (0x000000-0x1FFFFF)')
551        block = index // 0x8000
552        no = 0x40 + block
553        addr = index % 0x8000
554    else:
555        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported extended area for No mapping: {area}')
556
557    if addr < 0 or addr > 0xFFFF:
558        raise ValueError('Address out of 16-bit range')
559
560    return ExtNoAddress(no=no, addr=addr, unit=unit)

Encode an extended-area address into (No., 16-bit address).

This is the main helper for CMD=94-99 on areas such as:

  • ES, EN, H
  • U, EB
  • extended bit-device families when they are addressed by word/byte form

Note:

  • real-hardware FR word access uses encode_fr_word_addr32() with CMD=C2/C3, not CMD=94-99
def fr_block_ex_no(index: int) -> int: 
492def fr_block_ex_no(index: int) -> int:
493    """Return the FR block ``Ex No.`` for a word index.
494
495    FR is organized in 0x8000-word blocks. The manual's FR registration
496    command (`CMD=CA`) uses the block `Ex No.` in the range ``0x40-0x7F``.
497    """
498    if index < 0x000000 or index > 0x1FFFFF:
499        raise ValueError('FR index out of range (0x000000-0x1FFFFF)')
500    return 0x40 + (index // 0x8000)

Return the FR block Ex No. for a word index.

FR is organized in 0x8000-word blocks. The manual's FR registration command (CMD=CA) uses the block Ex No. in the range 0x40-0x7F.

def encode_fr_word_addr32(index: int) -> int: 
503def encode_fr_word_addr32(index: int) -> int:
504    """Encode an FR word index for PC10 block access (`CMD=C2/C3`).
505
506    Real hardware FR access uses PC10 block read/write with:
507    - high word: FR block `Ex No.` (`0x40-0x7F`)
508    - low word: byte offset inside the 0x8000-word block
509    """
510    ex_no = fr_block_ex_no(index)
511    byte_addr = (index % 0x8000) * 2
512    return encode_exno_byte_u32(ex_no, byte_addr)

Encode an FR word index for PC10 block access (CMD=C2/C3).

Real hardware FR access uses PC10 block read/write with:

  • high word: FR block Ex No. (0x40-0x7F)
  • low word: byte offset inside the 0x8000-word block
def parse_relay_hops(text: str) -> List[Tuple[int, int]]: 
23def parse_relay_hops(text: str) -> List[Tuple[int, int]]:
24    """Parse relay hops from `P1-L2:N2` or `0x12:0x0002` style text."""
25    hops: List[Tuple[int, int]] = []
26    for part in text.split(","):
27        item = part.strip()
28        if not item:
29            continue
30        m = re.fullmatch(r"P([0-9A-Fa-f])[-:]L([0-9A-Fa-f])\s*:\s*N([0-9A-Fa-fx]+)", item, re.IGNORECASE)
31        if m:
32            link = (int(m.group(1), 16) << 4) | int(m.group(2), 16)
33            station = int(m.group(3), 0)
34            if station < 1:
35                raise ValueError("N number must be >= 1")
36            hops.append((link, station))
37            continue
38        m = re.fullmatch(r"([0-9A-Fa-f])[-:]([0-9A-Fa-f]):([0-9A-Fa-fx]+)", item)
39        if m:
40            link = (int(m.group(1), 16) << 4) | int(m.group(2), 16)
41            station = int(m.group(3), 0)
42            hops.append((link, station))
43            continue
44        if ":" not in item:
45            raise ValueError("each hop must be LINK:STATION or P1-L2:N2")
46        link_text, station_text = item.split(":", 1)
47        hops.append((int(link_text, 0), int(station_text, 0)))
48    if not hops:
49        raise ValueError("at least one hop is required")
50    return hops

Parse relay hops from P1-L2:N2 or 0x12:0x0002 style text.

def normalize_relay_hops(hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> List[Tuple[int, int]]: 
53def normalize_relay_hops(hops: str | Iterable[Tuple[int, int]]) -> List[Tuple[int, int]]:
54    """Normalize relay hops from text or `(link, station)` pairs."""
55    if isinstance(hops, str):
56        return parse_relay_hops(hops)
57    normalized = [(int(link) & 0xFF, int(station) & 0xFFFF) for link, station in hops]
58    if not normalized:
59        raise ValueError("at least one hop is required")
60    return normalized

Normalize relay hops from text or (link, station) pairs.

def format_relay_hop(link: int, station: int) -> str: 
63def format_relay_hop(link: int, station: int) -> str:
64    """Format one relay hop in the preferred `P1-L2:N2` style."""
65    return f"P{(link >> 4) & 0x0F:X}-L{link & 0x0F:X}:N{station} (0x{link:02X}:0x{station:04X})"

Format one relay hop in the preferred P1-L2:N2 style.

def resolve_device(device: str) -> ResolvedDevice: 
185def resolve_device(device: str) -> ResolvedDevice:
186    """Resolve a string device address into a normalized access descriptor."""
187    prefix, area, unit = _infer_unit_and_area(device)
188    text = device.strip().upper()
189    if prefix is None and area in _PREFIX_REQUIRED_AREAS:
190        raise ValueError(f"{area} area requires P1-/P2-/P3- prefix: {text}")
191
192    if prefix:
193        ex_no, parsed = parse_prefixed_address(text, unit)
194        if unit == "bit":
195            bit_no, addr = encode_program_bit_address(parsed)
196            addr32: Optional[int] = None
197            try:
198                # Keep PC10 addr32 when the basic bit encoder can represent it.
199                # Upper prefixed ranges are still valid for program-bit access
200                # even if they are outside basic direct bit ranges.
201                addr32 = encode_bit_address(parsed) | (ex_no << 19)
202            except ValueError:
203                addr32 = None
204            return ResolvedDevice(
205                text=text,
206                scheme="program-bit",
207                unit="bit",
208                area=parsed.area,
209                index=parsed.index,
210                digits=parsed.digits,
211                prefix=prefix,
212                packed=parsed.packed,
213                no=_PREFIX_PROGRAM_NO[prefix],
214                bit_no=bit_no,
215                addr=addr,
216                addr32=addr32,
217            )
218        if unit == "word":
219            if parsed.packed and parsed.area not in _BASIC_BIT_AREAS:
220                raise ValueError(f"W suffix is only valid for bit-device families: {text}")
221            return ResolvedDevice(
222                text=text,
223                scheme="program-word",
224                unit="word",
225                area=parsed.area,
226                index=parsed.index,
227                digits=parsed.digits,
228                prefix=prefix,
229                packed=parsed.packed,
230                no=_PREFIX_PROGRAM_NO[prefix],
231                addr=encode_program_word_address(parsed),
232            )
233        return ResolvedDevice(
234            text=text,
235            scheme="program-byte",
236            unit="byte",
237            area=parsed.area,
238            index=parsed.index,
239            digits=parsed.digits,
240            prefix=prefix,
241            high=parsed.high,
242            packed=parsed.packed,
243            no=_PREFIX_PROGRAM_NO[prefix],
244            addr=encode_program_byte_address(parsed),
245        )
246
247    parsed = parse_address(text, unit)
248
249    if unit == "bit":
250        if parsed.area in {"L", "M"} and parsed.index >= 0x1000:
251            return ResolvedDevice(
252                text=text,
253                scheme="pc10-bit",
254                unit="bit",
255                area=parsed.area,
256                index=parsed.index,
257                digits=parsed.digits,
258                packed=parsed.packed,
259                addr32=encode_bit_address(parsed),
260            )
261        if parsed.area in _BASIC_BIT_AREAS:
262            return ResolvedDevice(
263                text=text,
264                scheme="basic-bit",
265                unit="bit",
266                area=parsed.area,
267                index=parsed.index,
268                digits=parsed.digits,
269                packed=parsed.packed,
270                basic_addr=encode_bit_address(parsed),
271            )
272        return _resolve_ext_bit(parsed, text)
273
274    if unit == "word":
275        if parsed.packed and parsed.area in _BASIC_WORD_AREAS | _EXT_WORD_AREAS:
276            raise ValueError(f"W suffix is only valid for bit-device families: {text}")
277        if parsed.area in _BASIC_WORD_AREAS | _BASIC_BIT_AREAS:
278            return ResolvedDevice(
279                text=text,
280                scheme="basic-word",
281                unit="word",
282                area=parsed.area,
283                index=parsed.index,
284                digits=parsed.digits,
285                packed=parsed.packed,
286                basic_addr=encode_word_address(parsed),
287            )
288        if parsed.area == "U" and parsed.index >= 0x08000:
289            return ResolvedDevice(
290                text=text,
291                scheme="pc10-word",
292                unit="word",
293                area=parsed.area,
294                index=parsed.index,
295                digits=parsed.digits,
296                packed=parsed.packed,
297                addr32=_pc10_u_addr32(parsed.index),
298            )
299        if parsed.area == "EB" and parsed.index <= 0x3FFFF:
300            return ResolvedDevice(
301                text=text,
302                scheme="pc10-word",
303                unit="word",
304                area=parsed.area,
305                index=parsed.index,
306                digits=parsed.digits,
307                packed=parsed.packed,
308                addr32=_pc10_eb_addr32(parsed.index),
309            )
310        if parsed.area == "FR":
311            return ResolvedDevice(
312                text=text,
313                scheme="pc10-word",
314                unit="word",
315                area=parsed.area,
316                index=parsed.index,
317                digits=parsed.digits,
318                packed=parsed.packed,
319                addr32=encode_fr_word_addr32(parsed.index),
320            )
321        ext = encode_ext_no_address(parsed.area, parsed.index, "word")
322        return ResolvedDevice(
323            text=text,
324            scheme="ext-word",
325            unit="word",
326            area=parsed.area,
327            index=parsed.index,
328            digits=parsed.digits,
329            packed=parsed.packed,
330            no=ext.no,
331            addr=ext.addr,
332        )
333
334    if parsed.area in _BASIC_WORD_AREAS | _BASIC_BIT_AREAS:
335        return ResolvedDevice(
336            text=text,
337            scheme="basic-byte",
338            unit="byte",
339            area=parsed.area,
340            index=parsed.index,
341            digits=parsed.digits,
342            high=parsed.high,
343            packed=parsed.packed,
344            basic_addr=encode_byte_address(parsed),
345        )
346    if parsed.area == "U" and parsed.index >= 0x08000:
347        return ResolvedDevice(
348            text=text,
349            scheme="pc10-byte",
350            unit="byte",
351            area=parsed.area,
352            index=parsed.index,
353            digits=parsed.digits,
354            high=parsed.high,
355            packed=parsed.packed,
356            addr32=_pc10_u_addr32(parsed.index, byte=True, high=parsed.high),
357        )
358    if parsed.area == "EB" and parsed.index <= 0x3FFFF:
359        return ResolvedDevice(
360            text=text,
361            scheme="pc10-byte",
362            unit="byte",
363            area=parsed.area,
364            index=parsed.index,
365            digits=parsed.digits,
366            high=parsed.high,
367            packed=parsed.packed,
368            addr32=_pc10_eb_addr32(parsed.index, byte=True, high=parsed.high),
369        )
370    if parsed.area == "FR":
371        raise ValueError("FR does not support byte access; use word access via PC10 block commands")
372
373    ext = encode_ext_no_address(parsed.area, parsed.index * 2 + (1 if parsed.high else 0), "byte")
374    return ResolvedDevice(
375        text=text,
376        scheme="ext-byte",
377        unit="byte",
378        area=parsed.area,
379        index=parsed.index,
380        digits=parsed.digits,
381        high=parsed.high,
382        packed=parsed.packed,
383        no=ext.no,
384        addr=ext.addr,
385    )

Resolve a string device address into a normalized access descriptor.
